races.race_name: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Light The Night Up
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who is proving consistent; creditable fourth in C&D handicap on reappearance; now 2lb lower and Jack Nicholls takes off a bonus 5lb; possibilities.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth over C&D on reappearance; now 2lb lower and Jack Nicholls takes off 5lb

runners.name: Galileo Charm
runners.spotlight: Has broadly consistent 7f AW form that features a success on penultimate start; could go well, assuming she takes to this new scenario.
runners.comment: Could go well, provided her consistent 7f AW form is transferred to new scenario

runners.name: Spacewoman
runners.spotlight: Has failed to transfer her consistent sprint maiden/novice form to two handicaps; enough to prove upped further in distance.
runners.comment: Has enough to prove upped further in distance

runners.name: Reem Rak
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo effort when second at Newcastle (7f, AW) behind an unbeaten filly who franked the form by following up in a handicap at Newmarket's Craven meeting; interesting.
runners.comment: Best 2yo effort when second at Newcastle behind a filly who has franked the form since

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: Gained her novice win on AW and kept to that sphere in handicaps since, 2l third at Southwell (7f) most recently; place claims, provided she's as effective back on turf.
runners.comment: Place claims, provided latest AW effort is transferred back to turf

runners.name: Adba
runners.spotlight: Not solid on AW form but brings possible sources of improvement, namely addition of tongue-tie and new surface; receives weight all round and could go well.
runners.comment: Brings possible source of improvement; could go well off bottom weight

runners.name: Ten Sixty Six
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in first couple of juvenile starts then took two regressive steps; turnaround is required on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Turnaround is required on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Valkyrie Storm
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of her five 2yo starts, including at Kempton (1m, AW) the last twice; reasonably solid contender on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Juvenile form suggests she's a reasonably solid contender on seasonal debut

runners.name: Box Clever
runners.spotlight: Took another step forward to win Salisbury maiden (7f) but has shown no further progress in two handicap attempts; needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: No further progress in two handicap attempts; needs to raise her game

runners.name: Laughterintherain
runners.spotlight: Good second at Lingfield (7f, AW) on final 2yo start, shaping as if 1m will suit; may take another step forward now handicapping; one to consider.
runners.comment: Good second on final 2yo start; may take another step forward now handicapping

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Dei Gratia Regina
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth of ten in 5f contest at Bath where she was an 11-2 shot; helps to set only a modest standard but may be capable of better with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Helps to set only a modest standard on Bath effort but may do better

runners.name: Quantum Swift
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with second of seven at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) where she was a 15-2 shot; recorded a modest RPR but looks open to progress, especially upped a furlong in trip (dam 6f winner).
runners.comment: Runner-up at Wolverhampton; recorded only a modest RPR but is open to progress
runners.owner: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner

runners.name: Bassrah
runners.spotlight: February foal; 110,000gns yearling; by Wootton Bassett; sibling to two winners, notably Private Signal (7f 2yo; 93); dam 7f AW scorer (94), closely related to 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Legatissimo; yard's 2yo record this term features a winning newcomer at Nottingham; interesting.
runners.comment: 110,000gns buy; yard's 2yo record this term includes a winning newcomer at Nottingham

runners.name: Dubai Charm
runners.spotlight: January foal; £90,000 yearling; from the first crop of Blackbeard and first foal of 7f winner (RPR 90) whose siblings include Group-placed 6f 2yo scorer Brussels; stable's 2yo record this season features a successful newcomer; possibilities.
runners.comment: £90,000 buy; stable's 2yo record this season features a successful newcomer

runners.name: Iris Olivia
runners.spotlight: March foal; Persian Force half-sister to 5.7f 2yo winner Buenos Dias (RPR 77); dam 1m scorer (88), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Lilbourne Lad; connections' 2yo runners this term include a filly who scored on debut; one to consider.
runners.comment: Connections' 2yo runners this term include a filly who scored on debut

runners.name: King's Prize
runners.spotlight: February foal; Too Darn Hot half-sister to 7f AW 2yo winner King's Trust (RPR 81); dam Listed-placed 1m2f scorer (106), out of 7f 2yo winner, is granddaughter of half-sister to Oaks runner-up Flight Of Fancy; yard's 2yo runners this season include a winning newcomer; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Too Darn Hot filly; yard's 2yo runners this season include a winning newcomer
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Lucky Orla
runners.spotlight: March foal; by Oasis Dream and first foal of multiple 5f AW winner (RPR 86) whose siblings include useful sprinter Aberama Gold; stable's first 2yo runner of the campaign; market instructive.
runners.comment: Oasis Dream filly; stable's first 2yo runner of the campaign

runners.name: Phalanx Nation
runners.spotlight: February foal; 40,000euros foal, £82,000 yearling; by Sioux Nation and first foal of maiden (RPR 53) half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Quiescent, from family of good milers Barathea and Gossamer; yard has made a great start (3-4) with 2yos this term; respected.
runners.comment: £82,000 yearling; yard has made a great start (3-4) with 2yos this season; respected

runners.name: Phoenix Moon
runners.spotlight: Has done all her winning over 6f on Lingfield AW, including on reappearance when getting up in the final strides; no certainty to follow up, being 0-7 on turf and 10lb higher than last attempt in this sphere.
runners.comment: All wins on Lingfield AW, including on reappearance; 0-7 on turf

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Best effort for current stable when close second at Thirsk (7f) in most recent turf attempt; still nicely handicapped, having recorded both wins (6f) off higher marks; commands respect back down in distance.
runners.comment: Close second in most recent turf start; remains nicely treated; respected

runners.name: King David
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-22 and he has been campaigned mainly on AW, mostly at 7f; worth another go over 6f and is the opposite of Phoenix Moon, being 10lb lower than last turf appearance; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Raced mainly on AW but is 10lb lower than last turf appearance

runners.name: Beaming Light
runners.spotlight: Completed a 1m hat-trick last June; form has dipped over 7f/6f/5f in last three outings and he has enough to prove in another sprint race; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Has enough to prove in another sprint race

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly over 5f, most recently 2l fourth at Nottingham last weekend; remains well treated, having gained both handicap wins off higher marks, and has two pieces of 6f form this year; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Finished 2l fourth at Nottingham last weekend; steps back up to 6f
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Big Apple Jack
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden in his first season last year, best form when making the frame twice over 6f; having been absent for six months, the market signals may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden in his first season last year

runners.name: Ishe Worth Agamble
runners.spotlight: Absent since a defeat 100 days ago that took his AW record to 0-5; three-time turf winner and ran well at Leicester on most recent attempt in this sphere; returns to a favourable scenario.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner; should be suited by return to this sphere

runners.name: Telling Time
runners.spotlight: Best form when placed twice on Wolverhampton AW; only midfield back on turf (good to soft) last time but may do better back on faster ground.
runners.comment: May do better back on faster ground

runners.name: Elashgar
runners.spotlight: Successful over 6f and keeping-on second over 5f at Wolverhampton kept to AW since handicapping; gelded since; remains open to further progress back up in distance; respected, assuming he's as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Remains open to further progress back up in distance on turf debut

runners.name: Faithful Dream
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but has several pieces of encouraging form, most recently third of 17 in cheekpieces at Nottingham (5f) last weekend; effective over 6f and has possibilities if taking well to visor.
runners.comment: Respectable third of 17 at Nottingham last weekend; possibilities

runners.name: Petes Diamond
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who is far from solid on form; achieved little on turf debut (7f) last time.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but is in form, fifth of 17 at Leicester (7f) returned to turf last time; could go well off the same mark.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but is in form, fifth of 17 at Leicester last time

runners.name: Noble Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on his AW maiden form but may do better now handicapping off a modest mark on turf.
runners.comment: May do better now handicapping and switched to turf

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on account-opening Chelmsford win kept to AW since and chance depends on the effects of this return to turf.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of this return to turf

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-18 and she looks fairly exposed, at least on AW; placed in two of her three turf runs (maiden/novice company).
runners.comment: Looks fairly exposed, at least on AW; placed twice on turf

runners.name: In Denial
runners.spotlight: Good fourth at Wolverhampton and hindered by wide trip at Chelmsford kept to AW since handicapping; may do better still at this sort of level; interesting, assuming she's as effective on turf.
runners.comment: May do better still at this sort of level; turf debut

runners.name: Ganthorpe
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has been campaigned mostly on AW, running well on penultimate start; siblings include a dual turf winner for his connections; interesting back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests he's interesting back on turf

runners.name: Bold Return
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has made the frame kept to 5f in her two handicap attempts, latest at Southwell (AW) on seasonal debut; in the mix, assuming she proves as effective at this new trip.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in her two handicap attempts; steps up to 6f

runners.name: Rupert Ritzik
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has achieved little; first-time headgear needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: First-time headgear needs to make a big difference
runners.owner: A Dearing, D Jenkins, T Bradshaw N Watts

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: Maiden who showed modest form for Aidan O'Brien and has even weaker claims on his AW efforts for new yard.
runners.comment: Maiden who needs to raise his game back on turf

runners.name: Sea Her Excel
runners.spotlight: Not totally solid on 2yo form but had wind surgery since and now goes in hood; those factors may prompt progress on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery since last run and now goes in hood; may progress

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Regressive RPRs in Southwell AW novice events; chance depends on how well she responds to new scenario and first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Regressive RPRs in Southwell AW novice events

runners.name: Layla Liz
runners.spotlight: Successful at Leicester on 2yo debut and solid record (2121) on Wolverhampton AW since, making all to beat Nad Alshiba Green on reappearance (5f) most recently; progressive filly who warrants respect.
runners.comment: Made all to beat Nad Alshiba Green at Wolverhampton on reappearance; progressive

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Green
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, including over C&D, for previous yard; close second to Layla Liz when favourite at Wolverhampton (5f) on AW/stable/seasonal debut, doing well having come from a difficult position; could well reverse the placings and go one better.
runners.comment: Close second to Layla Liz at Wolverhampton on stable/seasonal debut; has won over C&D

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Got up on the line at Southwell (5f) last time, taking AW record to 6-20; faces a slightly tougher assignment off revised mark back up in grade and back on turf; 2-10 in this sphere.
runners.comment: Southwell win last time took AW record to 6-20; he's 2-10 on turf
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Has returned to AW for two starts this year, better effort when runner-up on reappearance; has gained both turf wins at Windsor but is attractively treated off current mark; could go well.
runners.comment: Has gained both turf wins at Windsor but is attractively treated off current mark

runners.name: Smart Vision
runners.spotlight: Best to forgive reappearance effort (badly hampered); registered three wins apiece in 2024 and 2025 but needs to prove he can defy current mark.
runners.comment: Best to forgive reappearance effort but current mark looks difficult
runners.owner: J E Abbey And New Vision Bloodstock

runners.name: Michaela's Boy
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and it's four years since his sole success in this sphere; not the percentage call in this first grass attempt since last August.
runners.comment: Four years since sole success on turf; others preferred

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and has done all his winning over 6f/7f; inconsistent since last success; 0-12 over 5f and doesn't look the answer.
runners.comment: All wins at 6f/7f, mostly on AW; 0-12 over 5f

runners.name: Grandlad
runners.spotlight: Had an inconsistent spell on AW towards the end of 2025; has a better strike-rate on turf and recorded last year's August double off slightly higher marks; possibilities returned to this sphere on reappearance.
runners.comment: Recorded last year's August double off slightly higher marks; possibilities

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly in Bahrain since early 2025; far from solid on his domestic form since winning at Newmarket two years ago; no certainty to defy top weight.
runners.comment: Bahrain regular; far from solid on his domestic form since a win two years ago

runners.name: Muker
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner but reappearance record suggests he's probably best watched on this seasonal debut, unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Reappearance record suggests he's probably best watched

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Nottinghamshire Oaks Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: See The Fire
runners.spotlight: Smart filly who is a Group 3 and Group 2 winner at York (1m1f/1m2f); has plenty of Group 1 form, including placed efforts at Royal Ascot (against males) and Longchamp (close second) last season; takes on some unexposed and interesting rivals but sets a high standard for them to aim at; respected back down sharply in class.
runners.comment: Smart filly; Group 3 and Group 2 winner; has plenty of Group 1 form; sets a high standard

runners.name: Love Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo who has been kept to autumn races on soft/heavy ground; something to prove in this new scenario but otherwise an interesting type, having gained her 3yo success at Nottingham (1m) and being still open to further improvement; ran well in Listed contests the last twice and gives the impression she's ready for a crack at this new trip.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced; gained her 3yo win at Nottingham; open to further improvement

runners.name: Lava Stream
runners.spotlight: Absent since a 2024 campaign that ended with her coming up short in the Irish Oaks and Yorkshire Oaks; progressive earlier and went close in the Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot (1m4f) when bidding for a four-timer; could go well, provided she returns in good order.
runners.comment: Absent since a 2024 campaign that featured a close second in the Ribblesdale

runners.name: It's A Heartbeat
runners.spotlight: Irish filly who has very low mileage; completed a Curragh double (maiden/handicap) last summer; reappearance effort in Cork Listed event (kept to 1m4f) suggests this drop back in trip is worth exploring; may rate higher still; interesting.
runners.comment: Irish filly who has very low mileage; may rate higher still; interesting

runners.name: That's Amore
runners.spotlight: Similar type to Love Dynasty, being very lightly raced and one who may still be capable of bigger figures; fourth in the 2025 Cheshire Oaks is flanked by novice wins, latest at Southwell (1m3f, AW) on seasonal debut; likely has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Record is 2-3; improvement still possible; has a better chance than ratings suggest

runners.name: Alla Stella
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form when making the frame in Sandown Listed event and Curragh Group 3; one of two contestants who have been absent since 2024; needs improvement on ratings; market should offer further guidance.
runners.comment: One of two contestants who have been absent since 2024; check the betting

runners.name: Rock N Roll Pinkie
runners.spotlight: 3-10 at Nottingham, including the first of last year's three wins; effective at 1m6f/2m and ran well on recent return, so shortlisted.
runners.comment: 3-10 at Nottingham; effective at 1m6f/2m and ran well on recent return

runners.name: Macari
runners.spotlight: An excellent Flat campaign last year yielded three turf wins under Rose Dawes from May to October and he's been in AW/hurdles action this winter; absent 11 weeks but has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Excellent Flat campaign last year; in AW/hurdles action this winter; absent 11 weeks
runners.owner: R Vaughan, R Bisiker, M Jones And S West

runners.name: Just An Hour
runners.spotlight: Had 1m2f wins last April (here), June and July; kept his form well, including when second in October on his only attempt at 1m6f; recent reappearance was another creditable show here and he has each-way claims.
runners.comment: 2nd in October on his only go at 1m6f; recent reappearance was another creditable show

runners.name: Codiak
runners.spotlight: Raised his game for two 1m4f wins this winter and ran respectably in another AW race three weeks ago after 81 days off; a close call on turf on handicap debut (1m6f, good) last November gives plenty of encouragement that he will prove as effective moving away from AW this afternoon; in the mix.
runners.comment: Close call on turf on handicap debut (1m6f) last November before doing well on the AW

runners.name: Imperial Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Sole win (17 races) was a novice event in 2023; ended 2025 when bang there in two AW handicaps (2m/1m6f) and he wasn't disgraced behind Rock N Roll Pinkie on recent return, but not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Sole win (17 races) was a novice event in 2023, so he does not look the percentage call

runners.name: Spirit Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 1m-1m5f last January-August, the first three on the AW and the last on good ground; reappearance in March wasn't too bad but she was last of seven 18 days ago, which she needs to leave way behind her.
runners.comment: Reappearance in March wasn't too bad but she was last of seven 18 days ago

runners.name: Glint Of Light
runners.spotlight: Best effort in six outings for Hughie Morrison was her win in a handicap at Ffos Las (1m2f, good to soft) in August, the first of two starts in cheekpieces; first-time hood when pipped in 15-runner race at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) four weeks ago, which was a bright start under Ashley Lewis for new yard; up 3lb but a player if she's as effective on today's ground.
runners.comment: Close call at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft; 15 ran) was a bright start for new yard
runners.owner: E S-O Racing Club And Partner

runners.name: Shady Bay
runners.spotlight: Registered a 1m2f double on good to firm last July, with the first leg at this track; off the boil in the autumn (with cheekpieces added on final start) but one result is that she's now just 1lb above her last winning mark.
runners.comment: Off the boil in the autumn; one result is she's now just 1lb above her last winning mark
runners.owner: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell

runners.name: Solanna
runners.spotlight: Ridden by Rossa Ryan (now on Kurios Lady) on last two starts, prevailing narrowly at Kempton (1m3f) before a close fourth at Southwell (1m3f); they were on AW and he ran only once on turf last year, when a respectable third off this mark at Haydock (1m2f, good) last July but he needs better than that.
runners.comment: 1st and a close 4th on AW last two starts; ran only once (respectably) on turf last year

runners.name: It's Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh (1m1f) last April and went close here (1m, again on good) next time, but that form has not been repeated; well down the weights, so the second of his three runs this season offered some hope.
runners.comment: Well down the weights, so the second of his three runs this season offered some hope

runners.name: Kurios Lady
runners.spotlight: Four AW races, with fair form in her three this season; latest start was her closest finish when running on from towards rear to be sixth of 12 on handicap debut at Kempton (1m3f) early last month, beaten just over 2l; has the same mark today and something to build on.
runners.comment: Four AW races; ran on from towards rear on handicap debut (1m3f); something to build on

races.race_name: Sky Bet Proud To Support Young Lives Vs Cancer Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Dwindling Funds
runners.spotlight: Six wins last July-October (various ground) and campaign concluded with a near-miss over C&D; continued the good work when runner-up in a recent 0-95 here (1m1f, good) and he has leading claims back down in grade.
runners.comment: Six wins last July-October and reappeared with fine second here; leading claims
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: In good form on the Newcastle AW this year but not at the same level back on turf here (1m1f, good) a fortnight ago and now 0-11 on grass.
runners.comment: In good form on Newcastle AW this year but not at same level back on turf here recently

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Runner-up five times last May-August and he's on an attractive mark on that form; he may have needed last week's reappearance run at Ripon but was well beaten, which was also the case on his final few outings last season.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark but went off the boil at end of last season, and well beaten on return

runners.name: Eazy On The Eye
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on turf and hanging right on AW has been costly on her last two starts, being demoted from first at Wolverhampton (8.6f) then finishing third at Newcastle (1m; raced mostly alone); however, this front-runner arrives in good heart and has shown promise on grass; could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: 0-7 on turf but this front-runner has been in good form on AW & has shown promise on grass

runners.name: Madame De Sevigne
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but she returned to form with a close third at Lingfield (7f, AW) this month on her third stable start; she's fine at 1m and on turf and can be in the mix if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Back to form with close third at Lingfield and in the mix if backing up that performance

runners.name: Harry The Rogue
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from his 33 starts (both over 1m last spring on good/yielding) but he was highly consistent last year and ran another big race when going close on last month's stable debut at Newcastle (1m, AW); every chance he'll be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Just 2-33 but admirably consistent and went close on last month's stable debut

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Below par last October on last two runs for Dermot Weld and failed to threaten on recent stable/seasonal debut at Wolverhampton, but that run gives him something to build on and he's racing off his last winning mark today.
runners.comment: Reappearance run gives him something to build on and he's racing off his last winning mark

runners.name: Ashen
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and has come seventh on his three starts for this yard (7f/6f); however, he hasn't been running badly, with his latest effort coming in a big field at Thirsk, and there have been hints that his return to 1m may suit.
runners.comment: 0-10 but not running badly for this yard and it's possible the return to 1m will suit

runners.name: Swift Salian
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row last May/June (8.3f/1m1f, good/soft) and beaten just a head on this month's reappearance at Pontefract (1m, good to soft); this front-runner has a progressive profile and is one to consider today.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has a progressive profile and went close at Pontefract on reappearance

runners.name: Ayr Poet
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was as good as ever last season, winning three times, including at Ayr in September when last season; this two-time course winner is not ruled out on his return but there's a suspicion he'll improve for the run.
runners.comment: 11yo who won three times last season but there's a suspicion he'll improve for this run
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Two B Tanned
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won five times last April-July (good/good to firm), including over C&D; down the field at Nottingham last October when last seen but this mark isn't necessarily beyond her in view of her good third at Haydock (1m, good) in September; could make a bold bid if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Five wins last April-July and current mark not necessarily beyond her; reappearance

runners.name: Detective
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has an up-and-down profile last year and this is his first run since September; however, he was a good third over C&D (good) on his reappearance last April and won here (1m1f, good) in July; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: 10yo who returns from some time off but was third over C&D on his reappearance last April

runners.name: Native Honey
runners.spotlight: Last season's high points were his second at Doncaster (1m, good) in May and a win at Ayr (7f, good) in August; however, he's inconsistent and has to be at the very top of his game to win this on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Won last August but has to be at the very top of his game to win this on his reappearance

races.race_name: Enter ITV 7 For Free EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Seed Ya Later
runners.spotlight: Her dam is from a talented family and she recovered from a slow start to finish a clear second of 13 at 10-1 on last Friday's debut at Bath (5f, good); could be sharper for that run and she's a leading candidate.
runners.comment: Clear second on debut at Bath and could be sharper for that run; leading candidate

runners.name: Donna Rumma
runners.spotlight: Beaten 4l by the 11-8 winner on recent debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) but was sent off just 7-2 herself and would have finished closer with a clear run; the third has finished second since; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: 4l second on recent debut at Beverley and she'd have been closer with a clear run

runners.name: Pocket Change
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; fourth foal; Far Above half-sister to 2m1f/2m2f hurdle winner Lynches Knock; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 63), half-sister to 6f-1m3f winner The Blue Banana; a watching brief may prove best on debut.
runners.comment: 3,000euros yearling; half-sister to a hurdle winner; a watching brief may prove best
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 13 & P J Makin

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: 7,000gns yearling, resold £15,000 yearling; third foal; Caturra half-brother to 6f AW winner Fair Y Isle (RPR 71); dam multiple 5f winner (87), from useful family; it's likely he'll improve for this debut outing.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f AW winner Fair Y Isle (RPR 71); likely he'll improve for this debut

runners.name: Caturra Lights
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal, 42,000gns yearling; fourth foal; Caturra half-brother to 6f winner Brigid's Cloak (RPR 89); dam 1m/1m2f winner (78), half-sister to numerous winners; makes debut against rivals who have already shown considerable promise and he'll need to be useful.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f winner Brigid's Cloak (RPR 89); makes debut in warm-looking maiden

runners.name: Tamam Star
runners.spotlight: 27,000euros yearling; ninth foal; Kodi Bear brother to 6f/7f AW winner Night Narcissus (RPR 94), half-brother to four winners including Ascending (1m-2m4f; 107), Midnight Malibu (5f including 2yo; 99) and Mr Pocket (5f/6f including 2yo; 84); dam Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner (97); trainer has an excellent strike-rate here with 2yos and he's respected on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer has an excellent strike-rate here with 2yos and he's respected on debut

runners.name: Super Alpha
runners.spotlight: 8-1, no match for the easy winner when third over C&D (soft) three weeks ago on debut; however, he shaped as though he would improve for that experience and he has a classy pedigree; not ruled out.
runners.comment: No match for easy winner when third over C&D on debut, but likely to improve

runners.name: Stripes Of Glory
runners.spotlight: 10-1 when sixth of 11, beaten just over 4l, on recent debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft); her dam was a Listed-placed 2yo winner and that initial effort gives her something to build on; not discounted.
runners.comment: Her dam was a useful 2yo and her recent debut at Beverley gives her something to build on

runners.name: Epidavros
runners.spotlight: 0-12 last year but often ran well, including over C&D, and she reappeared with a solid fourth here (1m, good) a fortnight ago; has a chance.
runners.comment: Often ran well last season, including over C&D; solid fourth here on reappearance; chance

runners.name: Sovereign Bright
runners.spotlight: Convincingly beaten when third on both outings this year (1m/1m2f, AW) and she's 0-8; however, she's run just twice on turf and the first occasion produced her career-best RPR, when going close at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last June; the other run on grass was on heavy going last July; the return to turf could be a major positive for this 3yo.
runners.comment: 0-8 and needs to raise her game but the return to turf could be a major positive

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Continues to drop down the weights and ran okay when third in January last time; another 3lb lower today but she's been beaten on her 15 starts since winning in December 2024 on her second run; hard to be overly confident.
runners.comment: Continues to drop down weights but losing sequence up to 15; hard to be overly confident

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Two wins on AW towards end of last year (1m2f/1m4f, AW) and she's had a break since a lesser run in January; drops back in trip and needs to prove she's effective on turf, but she hasn't had much racing and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Improved form on AW late last year; not ruled out back on turf and back down in trip

runners.name: Salaria
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-7 since joining Charlie Clover when winning at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month; has also won over 1m2f on turf; disappointing favourite at Pontefract since but was restless in the stalls; may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Prolific since joining this yard; disappointing last time but was restless in the stalls

runners.name: Glorious Kitty
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but unexposed at this sort of trip; twice finished runner-up last October (1m/8.3f, AW/good) and reappeared with close second at Beverley (8.4f, good to soft) recently; she can be thereabouts once more.
runners.comment: 0-13 but unexposed at this sort of trip and went close at Beverley on reappearance

runners.name: Lady Dora Mae
runners.spotlight: Won Southwell novice (1m, AW) in January and followed up on handicap debut at Kempton (7f, AW); eighth of 12 at Lingfield (7f, AW) since but beaten only about 2l and this lightly raced 3yo may still have more to offer, with her pedigree hinting that this step up in trip may well be a plus.
runners.comment: Unplaced at Lingfield in hat-trick bid but the step up in trip may well be a plus

runners.name: Fille Imbassee
runners.spotlight: Won on nursery debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in January on final start for Marco Botti; however, she needs to produce more than she's managed this spring on her first two outings for this yard; turf debut.
runners.comment: Won AW nursery in January but hasn't kicked on for this yard subsequently

runners.name: Samra Star
runners.spotlight: Well treated on this year's AW form; however, she has an up-and-down profile and has a lower turf mark for good reason on previous evidence.
runners.comment: Well treated on this year's AW form but has a lower turf mark for good reason

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in nurseries last autumn and only seventh of 12 on this month's reappearance at Nottingham; however, she won a nursery at Ripon (1m, good) last September and could improve for her recent run; not written off.
runners.comment: Only 7th of 12 on reappearance but could improve for it; won Ripon nursery last September

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-9 when 6l seventh of ten on stable debut here (7f, good) a fortnight ago; however, she may have needed that run and she ran well at up to 1m2f when with Harry Charlton; interesting now back up in trip.
runners.comment: She may have needed her recent stable debut; interesting now back up in trip

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: Won at Ayr (1m5f, good) last July and this month's reappearance second at Catterick was over 1m4f; drops back to 1m1f here but she's shown versatility in terms of trip in the past and could again be involved at the finish.
runners.comment: 1m4f runner-up on reappearance; tackles 1m1f here but can be versatile in terms of trip

runners.name: Storm On Jura
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has raced only five times but she was well beaten last autumn in her first two handicaps; a transformation is needed on her return.
runners.comment: 4yo who has raced only five times but well beaten last autumn in her first two handicaps

races.race_name: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Close third at Beverley (8.5f) 11 days ago and she's won twice there at 1m2f, but her record at 1m3f-1m4f (six races) is less convincing.
runners.comment: Close third (8.5f) 11 days ago; two 1m2f wins but record at 1m3f-1m4f is less convincing

runners.name: Furhaan
runners.spotlight: Won handicap debut at Windsor (11.5f, heavy) in September and creditable fifth at Yarmouth (11.5f, good) the following month; left Ed Walker for 32,000gns in October and last week's reappearance (25-1) did not suggest an imminent return to winning form.
runners.comment: Last week's seasonal/yard debut (25-1) did not suggest an imminent return to winning form

runners.name: Night Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won off just 1lb lower at Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) last August, his second win there last year; 0-12 on AW but close third on latest start (first with tongue tied) was up with his best form; should go well.
runners.comment: Two 1m4f wins at Ascot (good to firm) last year; latest AW run was up with his best form

runners.name: Fast Fred
runners.spotlight: Second at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) on reappearance and better than ever when taking a 14-runner handicap over C&D (good) two weeks ago; 4lb higher this time but firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Better than ever when taking a 14-runner handicap over C&D (good) two weeks ago; up 4lb

runners.name: Hamlet's Night
runners.spotlight: Won on Kempton AW (1m3f; not proven over this far) in February and latest start was up with his best hurdles form; 0-13 on Flat turf but that includes five seconds.
runners.comment: AW win in February; good hurdles run latest; 0-13 on Flat turf but that includes five 2nds

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Made her 15th start, with tongue-tie and blinkers replacing cheekpieces, when getting off the mark in a nine-runner handicap at Dundalk (10.5f, AW) one month ago, doing it in clearcut style under 7lb claimer; has comparable form on good ground but not proven over this far (tried it on penultimate start) and she's 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 15th go when off the mark latest (10.5f, AW); 4lb out of the handicap and stamina unproven

runners.name: Motawaared
runners.spotlight: Down the field for George Boughey last May/June in his first two handicaps (1m4f/1m2f) and sold for 12,000gns in July; beaten further still at Ayr (1m) in September on his only start for new yard; moves back up in trip and needs to tap back into his initial promise.
runners.comment: Down the field in 3 handicaps, one for this yard; needs to tap back into initial promise

runners.name: Letsbefrank
runners.spotlight: Three wins (1m3f, 1m6f and 2m1f) in summer 2024 and he's 7lb below his last winning mark; struggled to get competitive last year until his running-on fifth under Lauren Young in a blanket finish on final start (1m4f, soft); reappearance record isn't great and today's test may be a bit too sharp for him.
runners.comment: Reappearance record isn't great and today's test may be a bit too sharp for him

runners.name: Eroico
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Irish maidens, taking minor honours on last three outings (1m2f/1m4f on softer than good); has since changed hands and left Noel Meade; improvement is probably needed on this British, seasonal and handicap debut.
runners.comment: 0-5 in Irish maidens, taking minor honours on last three outings (1m2f/1m4f); left N Meade

runners.name: Mister Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on reappearance at Newcastle (6f, AW) 30 days ago and needs to be an entirely different proposition today; however, he's on a competitive mark on last season's best efforts and his C&D form figures read 123, with all three of those runs coming in cheekpieces (which go back on).
runners.comment: Well beaten on reappearance but on a competitive mark and likes it here

runners.name: Abduction
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (7f, AW) in January and close fourth at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in February before winning over this C&D (good) a fortnight ago; it was a tight finish but he's gone up just 2lb; again in calculations.
runners.comment: Recent C&D win was a tight one but he's gone up just 2lb; again in calculations

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who is inconsistent but he won at Newcastle (7f, AW) in February and returned to form with a close third to Abduction over this C&D (good) a fortnight ago; he can be in the mix if again on a going day.
runners.comment: Returned to form with close 3rd to Abduction over C&D; in the mix if again on a going day

runners.name: Aisling Oscar
runners.spotlight: A revelation on AW for new yard since last November, posting an eight-timer; close second of six off this mark at Wolverhampton last month; he's 0-9 on turf but this is his first run on grass for his current trainer and he could be involved.
runners.comment: A revelation on AW for new yard since last November; first run on turf for current stable

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: Her mark continues to slide and she ran fairly well at Thirsk (7f, good) last week on her second start for Jim Goldie, who should get a win out of her at some point; however, she needs another step in the right direction today.
runners.comment: Ran fairly well on second start for Jim Goldie but needs another step in right direction

runners.name: Master Richard
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won twice in the second half of last year, the latest in a big field at Thirsk (7f, good) in September; went close on last month's reappearance at Newcastle (7f, AW) and he can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Went close on last month's reappearance at Newcastle and he can make another bold bid
runners.owner: T S Ingham & Mrs Liz Ingham

runners.name: Backspin
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW for Richard Hughes, for whom he won at Southwell last March, but he ran well when third of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) four weeks ago on his stable/seasonal debut; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Third of 16 at Doncaster on stable/seasonal debut and he's one to consider

runners.name: Little Empire
runners.spotlight: Running consistently well over 6f/7f on AW this winter and when a close fifth of seven at Lingfield (6f, AW) two months ago it was a tough Class 2; five runs on turf have yielded two wins and this Irish challenger is on the shortlist back down in grade and back on grass.
runners.comment: Running consistently well on AW this winter; 2-5 on turf and he's on the shortlist

runners.name: Royalty Bay
runners.spotlight: She's well handicapped on 2024 2yo form; last year wasn't devoid of spark but it was an underwhelming campaign and she has to raise her game.
runners.comment: Well treated on 2024 2yo form; not devoid of spark last year but underwhelming campaign

runners.name: Salam Dubawi
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who built upon his previous promise to win a maiden at Compiegne (1m, heavy) last September when last seen; sold for 35,000gns the following month; one of three for Jim Goldie; a market check is advised.
runners.comment: Won French maiden last September; sold for 35,000gns since; one of three for new trainer

runners.name: Persuasion
runners.spotlight: 9yo who hasn't been at the top of his game on either start this season but wasn't beaten far when sixth of ten over C&D three weeks ago and he's down to a tempting mark; won over C&D last April; possible contender.
runners.comment: 9yo; not at top of his game this season but this C&D winner is on a tempting mark

races.race_name: Sky Bet Build A Bet Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Senor Cortez
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (1m6f/2m) for Darryll Holland last August and made an encouraging start for his new yard when third to St Faz on Lingfield AW five weeks ago; the first of those wins was impressive over 1m6f on good to firm and he's one to consider despite today's 15lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Scored twice last August and made an encouraging start for his new yard five weeks ago

runners.name: Simple Star
runners.spotlight: Plentiful form at Musselburgh with three C&D wins, including last August for his only Flat win last year (4lb higher today); never dangerous when 50-1 for reappearance here two weeks ago but showed promise from out the back and could be a lot more potent this time.
runners.comment: Plentiful form here; promise on reappearance (50-1); could be a lot more potent this time

runners.name: Alther Walden
runners.spotlight: Runaway winner of four-runner novice at Catterick (upped to 1m4f, good to firm) last August; had wind surgery in November and a clearcut win when favourite for seven-runner handicap at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) five weeks ago; should stay and 6lb rise doesn't look harsh; well related and could easily have more to offer, so the hat-trick beckons.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; two 1m4f wins; 6lb rise doesn't look harsh; could easily have more to offer

runners.name: St Faz
runners.spotlight: Seen mostly over jumps in his Irish days; 3-3 on AW (1m6f/2m) last month upon joining this stable, before a lesser show three weeks ago when getting hampered at the start prevented his usual tactics; not ruled out on return to turf.
runners.comment: 3-3 on AW last month upon joining this yard, before he was hampered at the start last time

runners.name: Trojan Sun
runners.spotlight: 4-11 at Musselburgh, including three 1m6f/2m wins on good ground last May-October; also scored at Nottingham in October, a runaway win over 2m2f on heavy going which sees him 3lb higher today; soundly beaten at 40-1 here on reappearance three weeks ago and, judged on last season, it may take him another run before he refinds his form.
runners.comment: 4-11 at Musselburgh; soundly beaten at 40-1 here on reappearance three weeks ago

runners.name: Whimsy
runners.spotlight: Had three seasons with Andrew Balding, including three wins, and has been with this yard since July 2023, including for two wins (one over hurdles) and plenty of place returns; third over 1m4f in her only two Flat runs in the last 13 months, including under Patrick McGettigan 17 days ago, and her very solid Flat record for this trainer suggests she will be on the premises.
runners.comment: Very solid Flat record for this trainer suggests she will be on the premises

runners.name: Talismans Time
runners.spotlight: Won small-field races at Hamilton for a maiden (1m3f, good to firm) last May and handicap (1m5f, good) last July; seriously raised in class when tailed-off last at 14-1 in the Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm; again sweating) last August, but he could be of renewed interest on this reappearance for an in-form yard.
runners.comment: Last in Melrose at York (14-1 in hat-trick bid) last August; could be of renewed interest

runners.name: Mountain Road
runners.spotlight: 4-15 for David Simcock, including two wins in summer 2024, before tailed off that September on his only appearance for this yard; dropped just 3lb in his absence and probably best watched this time.
runners.comment: Tailed off in September 2024 on sole run for this yard; dropped just 3lb in his absence

runners.name: Mon Na Slieve
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent last year but won twice (5f, good), including over C&D, and he returned to form with a near-miss at Newcastle (5f, AW) last month; major player if he's once more on a going day.
runners.comment: C&D winner who went very close at Newcastle latest; major player if again on a going day

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good to soft) last September on sole previous course visit and reappeared with near-miss at Beverley (5f, good to soft) on April 15 before winning there (5f, good) on Thursday; did it a shade cosily last time and this progressive 4yo could take a 5lb penalty in his stride.
runners.comment: Did it a shade cosily at Beverley on Thursday and could take 5lb penalty in his stride

runners.name: Jer Batt
runners.spotlight: Without a win since August 2024 but well treated on last year's best efforts and he shaped when seventh of 14 over C&D three weeks ago as though he'd be sharper for that comeback run; seems to like it here; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: No win since August 2024 but well treated and seems to like it here; on the shortlist

runners.name: Montezuma
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who built on his reappearance second over C&D (soft) to win a 17-runner handicap over C&D (good to soft) a fortnight ago; also effective on good/good to firm; up 7lb for last time but did it nicely and he could be just the type of Jim Goldie sprinter to keep on improving.
runners.comment: Big-field C&D win this month; could be just the type of Goldie sprinter to keep improving
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Azuinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick last summer (5f/6f) and had excuses on his final two starts last season, namely a slow start at tight Chester and soft ground at Doncaster; could rediscover his progressive ways on this comeback.
runners.comment: Hat-trick last summer and had excuses on final two starts last season; makes reappearance
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Arnhem
runners.spotlight: He did well after joining this yard last year and he's on a handy mark; he should get back on track sooner or later but this 10yo has failed to shine on his last three outings, over C&D on last two starts.
runners.comment: On a handy mark but this 10yo has failed to shine on his last three outings

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: It's a long time since he won but he was a close third over C&D (good to soft) last September and was an encouraging sixth of 18 on recent reappearance at Thirsk (6f, good); might not be far away if the removal of the tongue-tie isn't an issue; won in 2023 when he last wore these cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Encouraging reappearance; might not be far away if removal of tongue-tie isn't an issue

runners.name: Ziggy's Triton
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last August and scored twice in a row at Southwell (5f, AW) in December; didn't run badly when fifth of 12 back on turf over C&D (soft) three weeks ago and could build on that back on better ground.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fifth over C&D (soft) three weeks ago and could build on that on better ground

runners.name: South Parade
runners.spotlight: She's dropped back down to a dangerous mark but she struggled on her final three starts last season and did likewise on her reappearance over C&D a fortnight ago; a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but struggled at the end of last term and did likewise on reappearance
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: His form can be up and down but he's gone close on two of his last four starts, most recently off this at Nottingham (5f, good) last month; has won twice over C&D and could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Went close at Nottingham this month and this two-time C&D winner could be in the shake-up
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Pop Star
runners.spotlight: His reduced mark appeals on form as recent as last September but he's struggled to get competitive on his six runs since December.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark but he's struggled to get competitive on his six runs since December

runners.name: Reigning Profit
runners.spotlight: Trouble in running on both AW starts last month and same story at Pontefract last time, in the circumstances doing well to finish eighth of 13 from 4lb out of the handicap; he has a good record at Pontefract but could play a leading role off his proper mark today if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Trouble in running at Pontefract latest; could play a leading role if getting the breaks

runners.name: Archduke Ferdinand
runners.spotlight: Course winner who has dropped to a mark 3lb lower than when winning at Redcar (6f, good to firm) last May; he could improve for this month's reappearance run over C&D but he failed to threaten, which was also the case on his final few outings last season; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: He's 3lb below his last winning mark but he needs to get back on track

runners.name: City Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Launched handicap career with two good AW runs this year; short of room at crucial stage before keeping on for fourth at Southwell (1m) this month and might have a bigger effort in him if this switch to turf suits.
runners.comment: Launched handicap career with two good AW runs this year, and may still have potential

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; shaped quite well before fading into sixth at Southwell (7f, AW) last month but raced too freely and was safely held when upped to 1m at Redcar (good to firm; behind Harswell River) on Monday.
runners.comment: Ran quite well over 7f last month but raced too freely when upped to 1m on Monday

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; ran poorly on seasonal debut ten days ago but looked unlucky not to finish closer than seventh at Redcar on Monday (1m, good to firm; denied clear run); not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: 0-9, but looked unlucky not to finish closer than seventh at Redcar on Monday; e-w claims

runners.name: Liquid Cooled
runners.spotlight: Finished well to make winning seasonal/handicap debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) in March, and this month's fourth of 14 at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) was perfectly respectable; has some stamina on his dam's side and looks interesting on this first attempt at 1m.
runners.comment: Finished well for 6f win on handicap debut; looks interesting on this first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Alice's Influence
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four starts and put in fairly good front-running shift before weakening into fourth on last month's seasonal/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW); harmed her chance by persistently hanging right there and may improve if the new visor helps to keep her focused here.
runners.comment: Ran quite well from the front when fourth on handicap debut; might be aided by visor here

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: Looks fully exposed after 16 starts but has performed well on both appearances this year, more recently when third of 12 at Redcar (1m, good to firm) on Monday; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Looks fully exposed after 16 starts but ran well when placed at Redcar on Monday

runners.name: Gloriola
runners.spotlight: Struggled in her first two handicaps, the latter on seasonal debut at Nottingham (8.3f, good) this month, and has now been unplaced all five starts; can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Struggled in her first two handicaps (6f/8.3f) and can't be recommended

runners.name: Truly Special
runners.spotlight: Placed in Leicester nursery (1m, good) for David O'Meara in October but signed off for him with down-the-field AW run in November and needs to rebound on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Placed in Leicester nursery for David O'meara in October; off since lesser run in November

races.race_name: Bet With The On-Course Bookmakers Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Princling
runners.spotlight: Lost out only narrowly to a next-time-out winner on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) in October and probably bettered that form when second to another good prospect at Newmarket (7f, good) later that month; upped in trip for this seasonal debut; ought to be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Runner-up to useful prospects on both 7f starts in October; holds strong claims here

runners.name: Oathbound
runners.spotlight: Quite promising debut when fifth of ten at Newbury (1m, soft) in October but disappointed at Southwell (AW) the following month; stablemate Princling appeals much more.
runners.comment: Made quite promising debut in October but second 2yo run was disappointing

runners.name: Unchartedterritory
runners.spotlight: Well-bred gelding who bettered low-key 2yo debut when respectable fourth of seven at Ayr (1m, good to soft) in September; should have more improvement to come this year but a minor role may be his best hope here.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth at Ayr in September; should have more improvement to come this year

runners.name: Cliff Danger
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam useful bumper and 1m6f Flat (RPR 101) winner for the stable, half-sister to winners John Clare (7f) and Special Mayson (6f/7f); has good standard to reach on debut.
runners.comment: Out of a useful bumper/Flat winner for the stable; has good standard to reach on debut

runners.name: Ervani
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; closely related to a French 1m1f/1m2f winner, half-brother to five winners including Eziyra (7f-1m4f including Group 2; RPR 114), Etneya (1m; 101) and Eshera (1m including Listed; 96); dam 9.4f/1m4f winner (99), half-sister to two Gold Cup winners; makes appeal on paper.
runners.comment: Out of a useful half-sister to two Gold Cup winners; makes appeal on paper

runners.name: Picture Palace
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on both 1m AW starts this year but no match for easy winner when second of four at Chelmsford on first occasion and beaten over 7l when fourth (promoted to third) at Kempton last time; gelded last month; improvement required.
runners.comment: Made the frame on both 1m AW starts but the form isn't anything to go wild about

runners.name: Freddie Steady Go
runners.spotlight: Created good impression when winning good-ground Newcastle bumper on debut last April but was very disappointing on hurdle debut there in January (had wind op soon afterwards); probably best watched on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Won a bumper last year but struggled on hurdle debut; best watched on Flat debut
runners.owner: Castle View, A Bell, S Oliver & M Severs

runners.name: Lorton Valley
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise when fifth in two 1m AW novice races in February/March but will probably be of greater interest when qualified for handicaps.
runners.comment: Displayed promise on both AW starts but run-of-the-mill handicaps beckon

runners.name: Helios Path
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on first two starts, more recently on seasonal debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) this month; drops back in trip here; cheekpieces removed after one use.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on first two starts

races.race_name: Vertu Mazda York Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sea The Storm
runners.spotlight: Trained as 2yo by David Menuisier (same owners); Sea The Stars filly who overcame evident inexperience to emerge well on top in a maiden at Sandown (1m, soft) in September on debut; 4-5 for Group 3 at Baden-Baden (1m, soft) five weeks later, which she didn't win but her running-on second of eight suggested she could be a very useful prospect and is far and away the best performance in this field.
runners.comment: Trained as 2yo by D Menuisier (same owners); won debut and second in a German Group 3

runners.name: Pierian
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; Palace Pier close relation to 1m2f winner Noble Horizon (RPR 95), half-sister to six winners including Thunder Drum (1m3f/1m4f including Group 3; 105) and Dubhe (1m-2m; 103); dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Irish Oaks; 118), sister to King George winner Nathaniel; lots to like there but, as stablemate of Sea The Storm, the betting should give all-important clues.
runners.comment: Lots to like on pedigree; as stablemate of Sea The Storm, the betting can give huge clues
runners.owner: Lord N P V J Rothschild

runners.name: Ceinture d'Orion
runners.spotlight: 210,000euros yearling by Sea The Stars; 11th foal; closely related to 9.4f-1m2f AW winner Demonstration (RPR 91), half-sister to five winners including Jouska (5f 2yo/6f Listed; 107) and Wasim (6.5f-7f including 2yo/dirt; 102); dam US 1m winner; faces a good rival on debut but respected on paper.
runners.comment: 210,000euros yearling by Sea The Stars; faces a good rival on debut but respected on paper

runners.name: Matilda Bea
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros foal, 8,000euros yearling; tenth foal; Australia half-sister to five winners, notably Nicklaus (6f-1m; RPR 99); dam German 1m-1m4f winner (including 2yo/Listed; 101); she'd have to be red hot on debut to beat an on-song Sea The Storm, though.
runners.comment: 8,000euros yearling by Australia; half-sister to five winners, notably Nicklaus (RPR 99)

runners.name: Nowshesdancing
runners.spotlight: Teofilo filly who made a winning debut when 18-1 for an eight-runner maiden at Dundalk (10.5f, AW) in January, slowly into stride and meeting some traffic but nevertheless coming from last to first; has since changed hands and left Mark Fahey; the form is nothing special but she finished to cracking effect in grabbing that win and has to be capable of better.
runners.comment: Debut form (10.5f, AW) nothing special but she finished to cracking effect to grab a win

runners.name: Wadacre Geisha
runners.spotlight: Has run twice this spring, improving at Beverley (8.5f, good to soft) 11 days ago but that was still a 15l defeat and low-level form.
runners.comment: Better at Beverley (8.5f) 11 days ago but that was still a 15l defeat and low-level form

runners.name: Lady Buttercup
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in September 2024 but 0-7 over hurdles, followed by two low-level performances under Flat rules; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Bumper win 2024 but 0-7 over hurdles, followed by two low-level efforts under Flat rules

races.race_name: Book Tickets Online At wetherbyracing.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Beyond The Bar
runners.spotlight: Made all to justify odds-on favouritism in Redcar novice (1m, good) in October on third start but ran poorly when upped to 1m1f for subsequent Newmarket nursery; gelded soon afterwards and now drops back in trip for this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: 1m novice winner last October; off since disappointing nursery debut over 1m1f in November

runners.name: Harswell Angel
runners.spotlight: Made a quite encouraging seasonal debut last month (6f, AW) but never really looked threatening when midfield at Newmarket (7f, good) 12 days ago and needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Ran okay on seasonal debut but never really looked threatening at Newmarket last time

runners.name: Supreme Clarets
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; largely consistent as a 2yo but made a low-key reappearance a fortnight ago (1m) and needs to have taken significant benefit from that outing.
runners.comment: Largely consistent as a 2yo but made a low-key reappearance a fortnight ago; now 0-7

runners.name: Sanaam
runners.spotlight: Came good at third attempt, with clear success in Southwell maiden (7f, AW; first run as a gelding) in December; not seen since but the form has been well advertised by the runner-up; one to consider.
runners.comment: Clear winner of Southwell maiden in December; form well advertised by runner-up

runners.name: Dacres Cross
runners.spotlight: Won five-runner Carlisle nursery (6f, good) last summer but his 2yo campaign fizzled out somewhat; moves up in trip for this reappearance; others have less to prove.
runners.comment: 2yo campaign fizzled out after 6f nursery win last August; up in trip for reappearance

runners.name: Back To Me
runners.spotlight: Left inauspicious debut a long way behind when winning 6f AW maiden in Ireland in November and followed up in Southwell novice (7f, AW) for new stable three weeks ago; has more on his plate in this handicap and needs to prove himself on turf but remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Good winner of AW novice on stable debut; this race is tougher but he remains unexposed

runners.name: Shahik
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for 55,000gns after winning a small-field Newcastle maiden (6f, AW) in October, his third start; unable to land any sort of blow in 7f AW Listed race for new stable in February but has sights lowered on this handicap debut; sports first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Safely held in a Listed race on stable debut but this handicap debut is more realistic

runners.name: Hasbro Market
runners.spotlight: Delivered on debut promise when winning Thirsk novice (7f, good to firm) last August and probably bettered that form when second in a quite valuable Ayr race (7f, soft) a few weeks later; makes handicap debut after seven-month absence (gelded in the autumn) but should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Second in valuable Ayr novice in September; makes handicap debut after seven-month break

runners.name: Evening Blues
runners.spotlight: 1m maiden winner at Dundalk (AW) for Ger Lyons in November, his fifth start; absent since second in a claimer over that C&D in December and, given his tendency to race too freely, removing the hood on this stable debut looks risky.
runners.comment: 1m AW maiden winner in November; off since second in a claimer in December; stable debut

runners.name: Gold Queen Kindly
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in two valuable AW handicaps at start of this year but this month's second at Southwell (7f, AW) was much better, albeit no match for the winner; probably needs another step forward.
runners.comment: No match for winner when second at Southwell this month; more needed

runners.name: Passing Thought
runners.spotlight: Perhaps a shade disappointing when upped to 7f for soft-ground Listed race on final 2yo start but won Hamilton maiden (6f, soft) in September and the form of her close second at Goodwood (6f, good) in August reads well; still has untapped potential over this trip and beyond; interesting.
runners.comment: Off since October but the form of last summer's Goodwood second reads well

runners.name: Art Gallery
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of her four starts, winning a 7f novice at Newcastle in December and adding a 1m handicap at Kempton four weeks ago; had more to spare than the winning margin of a neck might indicate on that occasion and a 5lb rise may not prove insurmountable.
runners.comment: Chasing a hat-trick after AW wins at Newcastle (7f) and Kempton (1m); solid contender

runners.name: Rosa Inglesa
runners.spotlight: Useful ability over 7f as a 2yo, winning a Chelmsford novice on her second start and finishing a creditable sixth in a big-field sales race at Newmarket when last seen; likely there is more to come and she is an interesting handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Promising at 7f last year; moves into handicaps with the potential for better

runners.name: Miss Lady Grace
runners.spotlight: Dictated a steady pace when winning a 7f maiden at Wolverhampton in February; last of seven on her handicap debut (6f, AW) last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Dictated when winning at Wolverhampton; last of seven on handicap debut; opposable
runners.owner: R H J Martin And Mrs J Martin

runners.name: Mereside Diva
runners.spotlight: Two good runs at Southwell preceded her Newcastle success in February (7f; blinkered first time); she hasn't run badly at Lingfield twice since, for all she has finished down the field, and this test may prove more suitable; key player.
runners.comment: Running well this year and returning to turf won't do her any harm; likely to go well

runners.name: Secret History
runners.spotlight: Didn't need to improve on her 2yo form to win a 6f fillies' novice at Chelmsford in January; returns to 7f with a hood fitted; brings untapped potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Didn't have to improve to win at Chelmsford in January (6f); clear potential now h'capping

runners.name: Folkene
runners.spotlight: Yet to kick on from her impressive winning stable debut last summer but she didn't run badly in a big-field handicap at Doncaster four weeks ago and that run should have tightened her up; could yet rate higher.
runners.comment: Sharper for last month's return to action; could yet rate higher

runners.name: In A Hurry
runners.spotlight: Ended her 3yo campaign with two lesser efforts but she had been on the up beforehand; this mark should be in range if she is ready to roll after 190 days off.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 quietly but this mark in range judged on her progressive form beforehand

runners.name: Come On Eibhlin
runners.spotlight: Often highly tried at two (made her debut in the Queen Mary) but did manage to win a 6f fillies' novice at Windsor; unplaced in both handicap runs and could probably do with some more weight off her back.
runners.comment: Unplaced in both handicap runs; may need more weight off her back
runners.owner: Hold My Beer, Gee Gees Racing & N Clark

races.race_name: Lives Streams On Racing TV Extra Handicap

runners.name: Harswell Ruby
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on her seasonal return when justifying favouritism in a 1m handicap at Thirsk 16 days ago (good ground); well backed on that occasion and a 3lb higher mark may not be beyond her.
runners.comment: Well backed when winning at Thirsk 16 days ago (1m, good); one to consider up 3lb

runners.name: Mount King
runners.spotlight: His last three wins have come over 1m at Pontefract but he finished down the field at that track on his recent return to action; that run could have sharpened him up and he is on a winning mark; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Needs to build on his Pontefract reappearance but that's not out of the question

runners.name: Sea Suite
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when comfortably seeing off 11 rivals in a 7f handicap at Southwell (AW) last month; solid fourth at Musselburgh since and he shouldn't be inconvenienced by the return to 1m; arrives in better form than many; cheekpieces given another go; major player.
runners.comment: Good 7f runs to begin his 4yo campaign; still improving and looks a major player

runners.name: American State
runners.spotlight: Took his record to 2-3 since a wind operation when keeping on well in a 1m handicap at Southwell three weeks ago; up 4lb but he has shown comparable ability on turf and should give it another good go.
runners.comment: 2-3 since a wind op and a 4lb rise for his latest Southwell success doesn't look excessive

runners.name: Dark Rosa
runners.spotlight: Shot back up the weights after an easy win at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in November; latest Bath run was after a 12-week absence and she may fare better today with her rider's 7lb claim useful.
runners.comment: May come on for her recent Bath 7th; Harry Vigors takes off a useful 7lb; not discounted

runners.name: Chesneys Charm
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than when seeing off 15 rivals in a 7f handicap (good ground) at York last summer; neither run this year points to him being the answer today though.
runners.comment: Not found his form in two runs this year; drawn widest and others are more appealing
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: West Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress as may have been expected following a 1m win at Southwell last March; dropped away to finish eighth of the nine runners on last month's stable debut at Newcastle (1m); enough to prove for now.
runners.comment: Didn't progress for Kevin Ryan and he needs to leave his stable/seasonal debut behind him

runners.name: Lever Up
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; bits of form last summer that bring him into the reckoning and he kicks off his 4yo campaign with a tongue tie fitted; betting useful back from 236 days off.
runners.comment: 0-10 but did show some promise last year; tongue tied for his return; betting to guide

runners.name: Hale End
runners.spotlight: He was winning for the fifth time when landing a 1m handicap at Southwell in December; not far short of that level in defeat on his next two starts; each-way shout if getting a good pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, the latest at Southwell in December; each-way shout back on turf

runners.name: Zarathos
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but regularly placed; down the field at Doncaster on his reappearance four weeks ago but drops in class today and should be sharper.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden but regularly placed; down in grade with his reappearance behind him
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: Now 2lb lower than for December's Wolverhampton success (8.6f; visored first time) but that is a result of some poor runs since; change of headgear not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but out of sorts in recent starts; new headgear not enough to tempt

runners.name: Dovey Moon
runners.spotlight: Good progress as a 3yo, winning 1m handicaps at Chepstow (twice) and Nottingham, all on good ground; starts this year off a tougher mark; can win more races but perhaps not this one after 193 days off.
runners.comment: Three 1m handicap wins this year; another career best needed after 193 days off

races.race_name: Its Ladies' Day On 4th June Handicap

runners.name: True Promise
runners.spotlight: String of good AW runs for current yard since December; his turf record is less compelling but he has been placed on good ground; others are preferred for win purposes.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat on AW for current yard; has a bit more to prove on turf

runners.name: Shavkat
runners.spotlight: Two solid efforts for Roger Varian last summer, winning a 6f maiden at Lingfield (AW) on debut before finishing third under a penalty at Doncaster (6f, good to firm); not so good on his stable debut at Lingfield last month (after nine months off) but he looks to be on a fair mark for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Not at best on stable debut last month but his opening mark looks manageable; considered

runners.name: King's Crown
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins at Newcastle over the winter; his form has cooled more recently but today's return to Class 5 company will help and the cheekpieces return after being discarded last time.
runners.comment: Gone off the boil of late but returning cheekpieces and drop back into a Class 5 can help

runners.name: Alpine Girl
runners.spotlight: Five-time turf winner; only 2lb higher than for her Redcar win last summer (6f, good to firm) and her two AW runs this year aren't the ones to judge her on (blindfold slow to be removed last time); leading contender.
runners.comment: On a good mark and she needs serious consideration now returning to turf

runners.name: Glory Hyde
runners.spotlight: Dropped 3lb in her two runs this year yet neither has been without hope, perhaps in need of the run on the first occasion and then poorly drawn at Doncaster last time; better effort expected today.
runners.comment: Dropped to a dangerous mark and neither run this year has been without hope; contender

runners.name: Without Flaw
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f handicaps at Beverley in the space of a week last July; went close off 1lb lower at Pontefract in October; ran well on last year's seasonal debut but just a suspicion others are better handicapped.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins last summer; ran well fresh in 2025; others perhaps better treated here

runners.name: Dorney Lake
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Catterick on Wednesday when he couldn't get into it after a slow start; he had been threatening something better on AW beforehand and this drop in class can help; hood returns; chance if breaking better.
runners.comment: Lacklustre at Catterick on Wednesday but on a dangerous mark; chance if breaking better
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Ingleby Archie
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who goes well fresh; 1lb lower than when seeing off 19 rivals at York last May (6f, good); should be in the thick of the action.
runners.comment: Starts his campaign off a handy mark and he has gone well fresh in the past; shortlisted

races.race_name: FWD Insurance Act Private Handicap (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: The Heir
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate but he's running well; had A Time For Us just behind when narrowly failing to get up over this trip at Happy Valley last month and he did well to be on the scene back there 11 days ago after starting slowly; every chance if breaking on terms.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but he's running well and a slow start was no help last time

runners.name: I Excelle
runners.spotlight: Has won a trial but wouldn't be the obvious answer on his 6f/7f form on the track.
runners.comment: Has won a trial but wouldn't be the obvious answer on his 6f/7f form on the track

runners.name: Top Throne
runners.spotlight: Reserve; C&D winner last March; 0-8 in the meantime but has been showing more off his reduced mark; wouldn't be a hopeless case if getting in.
runners.comment: Reserve; C&D winner last March; has been showing more off his reduced mark

runners.name: A Time For Us
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia; struggled in Class 3s but was bang there at this level over Happy Valley's 6f last month and again he's got a good draw, which wasn't the case last time (ran well considering); in the mix.
runners.comment: May have found his level as ran well two starts back and was poorly drawn last time

runners.name: Lucky Twenty
runners.spotlight: Comfortably his best run was the first when a shock winner over 5f at Happy Valley; very opposable on subsequent exploits.
runners.comment: Shock debut winner last summer but very opposable on subsequent exploits
runners.owner: 24/25 Jimmy K H Ting Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Packing King
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on from his encouraging debut but has since produced form figures of 114 in trials and Zac Purton now takes over on official debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Opposable on official form but has since produced form figures of 114 in trials; Purton on

runners.name: Prestige Hall
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but posted three consecutive seconds over C&D before another good run from a tricky draw 11 days ago at Happy Valley (finished well from off the pace); Zac Purton (aboard for last three races) has jumped ship; still considered.
runners.comment: Three consecutive seconds over C&D and latest Happy Valley effort is worth upgrading

runners.name: Matzden
runners.spotlight: 7f winner in Australia; ran okay over C&D on first two Hong Kong starts but hasn't offered a lot in his last two races; however, the drop in grade can only help and he's armed with a good draw.
runners.comment: Modest the last twice but the drop in grade can only help and he's armed with a good draw

runners.name: Lucrative Eight
runners.spotlight: A bit of a mixed bag in his trials but kept on well to finish within a length of the winner on his recent C&D debut; doesn't have the benefit of Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim this time.
runners.comment: Third on debut but doesn't have the benefit of Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim this time

runners.name: Akermanis Gold
runners.spotlight: Has shown some promise without being overly threatening and he's downgraded from Class 3s, albeit with a poor draw to contend with.
runners.comment: He's downgraded from Class 3s, albeit with a poor draw to contend with

runners.name: Brilliant Winner
runners.spotlight: Only tenth on official debut and has offered nothing in his subequent trials.
runners.comment: Only tenth on official debut and has offered nothing in his subequent trials
runners.owner: Chow Kay Yui, Kong Kin Kuen & Lau Wai Shing

runners.name: High Rise Victory
runners.spotlight: Has figures of 8543 in this year's trials and doesn't have the best of draws for official debut.
runners.comment: Has figures of 8543 in this year's trials and doesn't have the best of draws

runners.name: Navas G
runners.spotlight: Has made steady progress in his trials, finishing third and second in the last two; no surprise should he run well under the promising Nichola Yuen who claims her 10lb.
runners.comment: Has made steady progress in his trials, finishing third and second in the last two

runners.name: Gutsy Bravo
