runners.spotlight: Mixed record in a number of trials, winning one in February but down the field in two others since then; best watched.
runners.comment: Won a trial in February but down the field in two others since then; best watched
runners.owner: Gary Lau Sun Tao & Ronald Lau Sun Ting

runners.name: Beauty Gemini
runners.spotlight: Has beaten just two rivals in his two official races; has since won two trials but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Has beaten just two rivals in his two official races; has since won two trials
runners.owner: Yuki Kong Yuk Ki & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Grand Patch
runners.spotlight: Reserve; second (turf) and first (dirt) in his two trials here so clearly has plenty of ability.
runners.comment: Reserve; second (turf) and first (dirt) in his two trials here so clearly has ability

races.race_name: FWD Insurance Goahead Group Handicap (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Smiling Falcon
runners.spotlight: Some of his efforts this season, as when a close third over this trip in February, would give him each-way claims; he's well drawn to boot.
runners.comment: Well drawn and a repeat of his close third here in February would see him feature

runners.name: Special Hedge
runners.spotlight: Defied a higher mark (1m1f) in October but recent efforts have been disappointing and he needs a revival.
runners.comment: Defied a higher mark (1m1f) in October but recent efforts have been disappointing

runners.name: Amazing Fun
runners.spotlight: Nearly won over 7f here in January but some laboured performances have followed.
runners.comment: Nearly won over 7f here in January but some laboured performances have followed

runners.name: Aestheticism
runners.spotlight: Stuck on a lengthy losing run and it might be extended here unless able to raise his game; eyeshield tried last time is removed.
runners.comment: Stuck on a lengthy losing run and it might be extended here unless able to raise his game

runners.name: Amazing Award
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 1m at Happy Valley; no knocking his recent form at that track but it's a concern that his form figures at Sha Tin are 0977.
runners.comment: Running well at Happy Valley but it's a concern that his form figures at Sha Tin are 0977
runners.owner: Chan Kwok Yuen, Terence Lun Tin Yeu & Paul Li Cheuk Wai

runners.name: Flying Amani
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but he's up to winning races for sure if he can kick the habit of starting slowly; risky but his turn will come.
runners.comment: 0-7 but he's up to winning races for sure if he can kick the habit of starting slowly

runners.name: Honorary
runners.spotlight: Reserve; fairly well held in all his six races and may need even more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Reserve; fairly well held in all his six races and may need more help from the handicapper
runners.owner: Yip Yuk Kit & Dr Tommy Yip Chun Ming

runners.name: Circuit Marshal
runners.spotlight: Low mileage and this trip looks the way forward after closing for second here a month ago; could be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Low mileage and this trip looks the way forward after closing for second here a month ago

runners.name: Massive Glory
runners.spotlight: Mixed record in a handful of trials; drawn wide when midfield over 7f here two weeks ago; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Mixed record in a handful of trials; drawn wide when midfield over 7f here two weeks ago

runners.name: Big Return
runners.spotlight: Was running well for a maiden and he finally shed that status when overcoming trouble in running under Alexis Badel over C&D 20 days ago; the manner of that win suggests a 6lb rise should be manageable.
runners.comment: Up 6lb but he overcame trouble in running under Alexis Badel over C&D 20 days ago

runners.name: Jolly Brilliant
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and has been running as though he needs further than 7f, including when close up behind Turin Champions here in January and when third last time; if improving for the extra furlong then could well be involved.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and has been running as though he needs further than 7f

runners.name: Tycoon Express
runners.spotlight: No better than fifth in 6f/7f handicaps here and again he's dropped unlucky with the draw; his day will come and possibly at 1m, so one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: No better than fifth in 6f/7f handicaps here and again he's dropped unlucky with the draw
runners.owner: Brian Leung Hung Tak & Sophie Leung Lau Yau Fun

runners.name: Shamz
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; essentially disappointing in Hong Kong but last run suggests he might be closing in on a competitive mark and he's dropped into a Class 4 here after winning a trial; drawn wide but not without a chance.
runners.comment: Downgraded and last run suggests he might be closing in on a competitive mark

runners.name: Grand Turbo
runners.spotlight: Reserve; nothing positive to take out of his first three races.
runners.comment: Reserve; nothing positive to take out of his first three races

runners.name: Family Knight
runners.spotlight: Won on the AW in Britain but hasn't been running like a winner waiting to happen in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Won on the AW in Britain; hasn't been running like a winner waiting to happen in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Stephanie Fung Wing Man, Kelly Fung Et Al

runners.name: Turin Champions
runners.spotlight: Was running well at this level despite some bad luck on the draw front, and also in running here two runs back; latest defeat was in a Class 3; back at the right level here, well drawn and Zac Purton takes over; lots to like.
runners.comment: Back at the right level here, well drawn and Purton takes over; lots to like
runners.owner: Dr Sheldon Tse Hiu Tung, Tsoi Huk Yam & Ken Huang Jian Hua

runners.name: Better And Better
runners.spotlight: Consistent enough and made the most of a reduced mark over C&D four weeks ago, though prevailed only narrowly; back up 6lb; has won a trial since.
runners.comment: Prevailed only narrowly here last month and he's back up 6lb

runners.name: Goldentronicmighty
runners.spotlight: All three wins over C&D; nothing wrong with his mark but has been a bit disappointing back at this level in his last two races; only sixth of eight in a trial since.
runners.comment: On a good mark but has been a bit disappointing back at this level in his last two races

runners.name: Hayday
runners.spotlight: All four wins at 6f, three of those at Happy Valley; has the form to figure at this level but latest run needs forgiving.
runners.comment: Has the form to figure at this level but latest run needs forgiving

runners.name: Solid Car
runners.spotlight: A beaten favourite here over 7f on last official run but he has competitive form in stronger races than this and returning to 6f looks a good move; also ran second in his latest trial.
runners.comment: Competitive form in stronger races than this and returning to 6f looks a good move

runners.name: Quartz Legend
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 2-3 in Australia; yet to strike in Hong Kong but was only 2l away over Happy Valley's 6f recently when made favourite; Joao Moreira rides again.
runners.comment: Reserve; only 2l away over Happy Valley's 6f recently when sent off favourite

runners.name: Oldtown
runners.spotlight: Only 3lb higher than for his C&D win in October; up and down since but perhaps a change of trainer can get him going again.
runners.comment: Only 3lb higher than for his C&D win in October; up and down since; new yard

runners.name: Mr Incredible
runners.spotlight: Made rapid headway from off the pace to make successful official debut over C&D in March but there were no apparent excuses when only sixth off this 7lb higher mark 20 days ago.
runners.comment: Debut winner but no apparent excuses when only sixth off this 7lb higher mark 20 days ago

runners.name: Absolute Heart
runners.spotlight: Has won barrier trials on either side of his C&D win last month when making all and beating solid yardstick Prestige Hall who runs earlier; looks promising and he's ideally drawn to employ the same tactics.
runners.comment: Has won barrier trials on either side of his C&D win last month when making all

runners.name: Majestic Life
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been shaping like a winner-in-waiting and again he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Hasn't been shaping like a winner-in-waiting and again he's drawn wide

runners.name: Precision Mind
runners.spotlight: Has won trials but went off 29-1 for official debut here over 5f and was never threatening.
runners.comment: Has won trials but went off 29-1 for official debut here over 5f and was never threatening
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Allan Chau Kit Ho And Mr & Mrs Chau Man Kwok

runners.name: Lucky Doctor
runners.spotlight: Reserve; a winner on soft ground in Australia but hasn't found his feet in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Reserve; a winner on soft ground in Australia but hasn't found his feet in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Dr Bosco Chan, Dr Chu Wai Ming Et Al

runners.name: Classic Triple
runners.spotlight: Has won a trial but finished last in his final one and that was also the case on official debut.
runners.comment: Has won a trial but finished last in final one and that also the case on official debut

runners.name: Jackson Habit
runners.spotlight: First and fourth in two trials since bringing up the rear over 5f at Happy Valley; could have improved since then and he's raised in trip.
runners.comment: First and fourth in two trials since bringing up the rear over 5f at Happy Valley

runners.name: Apex Glory
runners.spotlight: Successful in one of his three trials but likely that he'll need more on this official debut.
runners.comment: Successful in one of his three trials but likely that he'll need more on official debut

runners.name: Happy Promise
runners.spotlight: Figures of 132 in his last three trials so brings some potential to this official first start; betting can guide.
runners.comment: Figures of 132 in his last three trials so brings some potential to official first start

runners.name: Massive Reward
runners.spotlight: Sufficient promise in his trials to believe he has a future but wouldn't be the obvious answer on debut.
runners.comment: Sufficient promise in trials to believe he has a future but wouldn't be the obvious answer

runners.name: Star Figure
runners.spotlight: Often gets going too late in his races no matter what the trip, and he never got going at all last time at Happy Valley; too risky.
runners.comment: Often gets going too late in his races no matter what the trip; below best last time

runners.name: California Bay
runners.spotlight: Thereabouts plenty of times this season but this 17-race maiden was beaten when hampered here 20 days ago; not one for maximum faith even when handed kinder draws than today's.
runners.comment: Not one for maximum faith even when handed kinder draws than today's

runners.name: Nyx Gluck
runners.spotlight: 1-9 around here, the win coming over 7f in December; pretty solid in the meantime but gives the impression 1m is worth a go, which was the intention here recently only to be balloted out; respected nonetheless.
runners.comment: 7f winner here in December; now gives the impression that he needs 1m

runners.name: Kyrus Treasure
runners.spotlight: Reserve; some promise for sure but struggled in trials before bringing up the rear here 20 days ago.
runners.comment: Reserve; some promise but struggled in trials before bringing up the rear here 20 days ago

runners.name: Star Mac
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old and inconsistency has crept into this game; has never been the easiest to win with and has first official run (yard debut) since December on the back of some just okay runs in trials.
runners.comment: Not the force of old and inconsistency has crept into this game; beaten in recent trials

runners.name: Phoenix Light
runners.spotlight: Form figures offer little encouragement but he's either been poorly drawn or endured traffic issues; could go well this time.
runners.comment: He's either been poorly drawn or endured traffic issues on his recent starts

runners.name: Ace
runners.spotlight: It was a Class 5 in which he comfortably justified favouritism over C&D four weeks ago and he's back up 8lb; that rise spells danger on the balance of his form and he's poorly drawn.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner; 8lb rise spells danger on balance of his form; drawn wide

runners.name: Alabama Song
runners.spotlight: Reserve; had been running well prior to defying stamina doubts over this far here two weeks ago; didn't win with much in hand and he's up 5lb.
runners.comment: Reserve; didn't win with much in hand here recently and he's up 5lb

runners.name: Daily Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Both official runs have been here over 6f; very likeable debut and when a 4l ninth a month ago he didn't enjoy the clearest of runs up the straight; has since won a trial under Joao Moreira; unexposed and interesting.
runners.comment: Likeable debut and got no luck next time; has since won a trial under Joao Moreira
runners.owner: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho

runners.name: Poet's Reign
runners.spotlight: Nearly caused 70-1 surprise over 5f here on debut but beat only one home upped to 6f last month, albeit from a bad draw; subsequent trial efforts not overly encouraging but probably retains some potential and Oisin Murphy takes over (from a good draw).
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from encouraging debut but there's time yet; Oisin Murphy rides

runners.name: King Dance
runners.spotlight: Got going in the nick of time to win over 6f here on dirt and it was no surprise to see him improve for 7f when again leaving it late here four weeks ago; raised 7lb for that but open to further improvement at this trip.
runners.comment: Late player; 7lb higher than for recent C&D win but he's unexposed over this far

runners.name: Warriors Dream
runners.spotlight: Ready winner over 6f here last month but the 6lb rise found him out on his return visit; confined to sprinting in Hong Kong and yet he did win over 7f in Britain, so this far is worth a go.
runners.comment: Might be on a tough mark but this return to 7f is well worth exploring
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung

runners.name: Robot Star
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in January off 7lb lower and has continued to run well, twice at 1m and back over 7f last time; appeals as a likely contender for all that stall 10 spells danger.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January off 7lb lower and has continued to run well

runners.name: Lucky Balerion
runners.spotlight: Won his latest trial but beaten 5l and 11l in two official runs over C&D; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won his latest trial but beaten 5l and 11l in two runs over C&D; others appeal more

runners.name: Joltin
runners.spotlight: Promising when winning in Ireland; hasn't beaten many rivals in Hong Kong and remains best watched despite this drop in grade, especially from stall 12.
runners.comment: Promising when winning in Ireland; hasn't beaten many rivals in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Jack Chan Hoi, Brian Pong Chung Bon & Xin Yi

runners.name: Green Angel
runners.spotlight: Most competitive run in this year's trials was his latest but he's one to watch on debut.
runners.comment: Most competitive run in this year's trials was his latest but he's one to watch on debut

runners.name: Ka Ying Rising
runners.spotlight: Eight-time Group 1 winner who extended his winning sequence to 19 with fairly easy success in C&D Group 2 three weeks ago; already one of the all-time greats in Hong Kong, and cannot be opposed.
runners.comment: Already one of the all-time greats in Hong Kong, and can extend his winning streak to 20

runners.name: Comanche Brave
runners.spotlight: Irish-trained colt with 2-11 strike-rate; comes here after keeping on for third in 7f Group 2 in Saudi Arabia in February but is not sure to be suited by this drop back in trip and has a bit to find on the figures.
runners.comment: Kept on for third in 7f Group 2 in Saudi Arabia; drop back in trip not sure to suit

runners.name: Helios Express
runners.spotlight: Placed several times behind Ka Ying Rising since start of last season, most recently when second in C&D Group 2 three weeks ago; 5lb worse off with him today but ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: Placed several times behind Ka Ying Rising since start of last season

runners.name: Fast Network
runners.spotlight: Made the frame behind Ka Ying Rising three consecutive over C&D (twice at Group 1 level) before comfortably winning Class 1 handicap last month; not seen to same effect over 1m three weeks ago but considered each-way on this return to sprinting.
runners.comment: Twice in the frame behind Ka Ying Rising in C&D Group 1 races this season; place claims

runners.name: Raging Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Belied triple-digit odds when keeping on to take second in Group 1 Hong Kong Sprint in December and again ran well when third in C&D Group 2 this month but another minor role is probably his best hope.
runners.comment: Second at triple-digit odds in Hong Kong Sprint; another minor role his best hope, though

runners.name: Beauty Waves
runners.spotlight: Did well to win Happy Valley handicap from wide stall in February but is not the force of old and can be overlooked today.
runners.comment: Not the force of old and can be overlooked today

runners.name: Satono Reve
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f Group winner in native Japan and lost out only narrowly in 6f Group 1 at Royal Ascot last June; brushed aside by Ka Ying Rising when second in this race last year and didn't fire on latest visit to Hong Kong in December but was seemingly back in peak form last month and has a good chance to claim a minor prize.
runners.comment: Dual 6f Group 1 winner in native Japan; has good chance to claim a minor prize

runners.name: Tomodachi Kokoroe
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning C&D Group 2 handicap in October; ran well behind Fast Network two starts ago but was safely held in Group 2 won by Ka Ying Rising this month and faces another tough assignment.
runners.comment: Safely held in C&D Group 2 this month and faces another tough assignment

runners.name: Lucky Candy
runners.spotlight: Struggled initially (6f/1m) and his improvement has come over 5f, albeit with strong finishes at that trip; this far is certainly worth exploring and the potential is there to improve; his draw is offputting, however.
runners.comment: His improvement has come over 5f, albeit with strong finishes at that trip
runners.owner: Yeung Wai Kwong & Lee Sheung Ping

runners.name: Celestial Hero
runners.spotlight: Never emerged from behind over 6f at Happy Valley last time but had been running well here in the cheekpieces that now return; he's always dangerous if getting a strong pace to chase.
runners.comment: Last run needs forgiving but he's always dangerous if getting a strong pace to chase
runners.owner: Dr David Chain Chi Woo, Bernard Chain S Chiang & C C S Liang

runners.name: Lifeline Express
runners.spotlight: Both wins over C&D; not everything went like clockwork last time and every chance on his previous close second to Cool Boy as he's 6lb better off with that rival; from stall 2 there's plenty of positives.
runners.comment: Latest run underwhelming but every chance on his previous close second to Cool Boy

runners.name: Sugar Sugar
runners.spotlight: This is a hard to race in which to bounce back from two below-par efforts and Happy Valley is more his scene.
runners.comment: Well below his best of late and Happy Valley is more his scene

runners.name: Chateauneuf
runners.spotlight: Poorly drawn and recent efforts have been pretty substandard, so there have to be reservations despite this relegation into a Class 3.
runners.comment: Modest of late and drawn wide; reservations despite this relegation into a Class 3

runners.name: Circuit Grand Slam
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in November off 8lb lower and Britney Wong claimed 7lb that day; has shown he can deal with this mark and this is a better fit than his recent races in stronger company and over longer trips; respected.
runners.comment: This is a better fit than his recent races in stronger company and over longer trips

runners.name: Mickley
runners.spotlight: Reserve; Royal Ascot winner at 1m and probably better at that trip or 7f; does have competitive form over 6f this year but latest run was a bit of a blip.
runners.comment: Reserve; Royal Ascot winner at 1m and probably better at that trip or 7f

runners.name: Geneva
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who has returned from an absence in good form and plenty went wrong in the race before finishing well for second last time; has the same career-high mark to deal with but one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Plenty went wrong in the race before finishing well for second last time

runners.name: Thunder Kit
runners.spotlight: His last three races have been quite encouraging but he appears to have no great margin for error off this mark, which is down only 1lb; hood added.
runners.comment: Lightly raced but appears to have no great margin for error off this mark
runners.owner: Ian Chan Yau Nam & Cyrus Chan Sing Kit

runners.name: Pegas
runners.spotlight: Not ideally drawn for one who likes to be up there but arrives here in a solid run form and he also won a recent trial; hard to discount.
runners.comment: Not ideally drawn for one who likes to be up there but arrives here in a solid run form

runners.name: Outgate
runners.spotlight: 5lb higher than for his C&D win in February and struggled since, particularly on last two starts; the joint-oldest runner here at the age of seven.
runners.comment: 5lb higher than for his C&D win in February and struggled since

runners.name: Grit Spirit
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner in Australia; finished last on Hong Kong debut after an absence and again struggled in a trial this month.
runners.comment: Last on Hong Kong debut after an absence and again struggled in a trial

runners.name: Flying Hunter
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia but didn't offer a great deal in trials prior to finishing last on official Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: Didn't offer a great deal in trials prior to finishing last on official HK debut

runners.name: Magnemite
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 7f maiden winner in Australia; absent for a year prior to a quiet Hong Kong debut here last week.
runners.comment: Reserve; absent for a year prior to a quiet Hong Kong debut here last week

runners.name: Savvy Brilliant
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner on a competitive mark but he was virtually pulled up by Zac Purton on his last official start and only fifth in two trials since.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature but current wellbeing is an issue all of a sudden

runners.name: Cool Boy
runners.spotlight: Looked seriously progressive in completing his hat-trick over C&D and he got behind from stall 10 when a never-dangerous fourth here a month ago; ran well in the circumstances but has a similar scenario to overcome here; Zac Purton rides again, as was also the case when winning a recent trial.
runners.comment: Progressive and did well in the circumstances here last time; again drawn wide
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

runners.name: Voyage Bubble
runners.spotlight: Six-time Group 1 winner, most recently in Hong Kong Mile over C&D in December; bounced back from disappointing effort when third behind Lucky Sweynesse and My Wish in C&D Group 2 three weeks ago, and is 5lb better off with both today; good chance, despite awkward draw.
runners.comment: Six-time Group 1 winner, most recently in Hong Kong Mile over C&D in December; big player

runners.name: Lucky Sweynesse
runners.spotlight: Top-class 6f/7f performer in his prime (four Group 1 wins); not quite as good as he was and inconsistency has crept in but he held on gamely to pip My Wish in C&D Group 2 three weeks ago and won't be far away if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Not quite as good as he was but held on gamely to pip My Wish in C&D Group 2 this month
runners.owner: Cheng Ming Leung, Cheng Yu Tung Et Al

runners.name: Jantar Mantar
runners.spotlight: Top-class Japanese miler with four Group 1 wins on his CV; absent since November and flopped badly on only previous visit to Hong Kong (in 2024) but still needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Top-class Japanese miler with four Group 1 wins on his CV; taken very seriously

runners.name: Docklands
runners.spotlight: Gained his Group 1 win in last year's 1m Queen Anne at Royal Ascot; kept on for creditable fourth behind Voyage Bubble in Hong Kong Mile in December and returned from break with comfortable Listed win last month; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at Royal Ascot in 2025; fourth in Hong Kong Mile in December; e-w claims

runners.name: My Wish
runners.spotlight: Began this season with two emphatic wins, the latter in C&D Group 2 handicap in October; his improvement seems to have levelled off since but he lost out only narrowly to Lucky Sweynesse in C&D Group 2 this month, and has place claims again.
runners.comment: Lost out only narrowly to Lucky Sweynesse in C&D Group 2 this month; place claims again
runners.owner: Ada Che Xiao Hong, Suki Tang Xianfang & Ruby Hui Like Sea

runners.name: Galaxy Patch
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D Group 2 winner, latterly in November; gave good account when fourth behind Lucky Sweynesse in C&D Group 2 three weeks ago but is drawn wide today and others look stronger.
runners.comment: Sound effort when fourth in C&D Group 2 this month but others look stronger

runners.name: Sunlight Power
runners.spotlight: Excelled himself when close second to Galaxy Patch in C&D Group 2 in November but subsequent form has been rather mixed, and he managed only eighth in C&D Group 2 won by Lucky Sweynesse this month.
runners.comment: Excelled himself when second in Group 2 in November but subsequent form is mixed

runners.name: Red Lion
runners.spotlight: Caused 90-1 shock when clinging on narrowly to deny Voyage Bubble in this race in 2025 but has looked out of sorts this year (three runs) and needs a huge boost from the first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Shock winner of this race in 2025 but out of sorts this year; blinkers tried
runners.owner: The Hon Ronald Arculli GBM GBS JP & Johanna K J Arculli BBS

runners.name: Chancheng Glory
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when sixth in C&D Group 2 won by Lucky Sweynesse this month but has not been at best his since finishing second in 1m1f dirt Group 3 in South Korea in September and looks up against it today.
runners.comment: Second in 1m1f dirt Group 3 in South Korea in Septembr but up against it today

runners.name: Invincible Ibis
runners.spotlight: Rapidly progressive since cheekpieces were added in September, collecting four handicap wins (7f-1m) before last month's clearcut success in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f); has more on his plate today but might still be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Clearcut winner of last month's Hong Kong Derby; may still be on the upgrade

runners.name: Stormy Grove
runners.spotlight: Reserve; improved to cause 21-1 surprise in Classic Cup here (1m1f) last month and was placed behind Invincible Ibis in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) three weeks later; needs to progress again today, though.
runners.comment: Placed in last month's Hong Kong Derby but needs further progress today
runners.owner: Lily Chan Li Lei & Marc Chan

runners.name: Cap Ferrat
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of last season's Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) and subsequently placed behind Voyage Bubble in 1m4f Group 1; very little has gone to plan during light campaign this season, though, and he'd appeal more over further.
runners.comment: Last season's Hong Kong Derby here; very little has gone to plan this term, though

runners.name: Little Paradise
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps (6f-7f) this season and encountered some traffic before quickening clear for ready success in Classic Mile over C&D in February; two lesser efforts have followed but he may still have potential and Zac Purton takes the ride.
runners.comment: Impressive winner of Classic Mile; two lesser efforts followed; may still have potential

runners.name: Patch Of Theta
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won 7f Group 3 handicap at start of 2025 and was also successful in Class 1 handicap over that trip in February but 1m appears to stretch him and he faces a tall order today.
runners.comment: Won 7f Group 3 handicap at start of 2025 but 1m appears to stretch him

runners.name: Copartner Prance
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with narrow 7f Group 3 handicap win and was runner-up in two Group races in early part of this campaign but his form has dipped since and this is a very tough task, even if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Form has dipped since the autumn and he can't be recommended at this level

runners.name: Strauss
runners.spotlight: Japanese raider who posted career-best performance when winning Listed Abu Dhabi Gold Cup over 1m in UAE in February; contests a stronger race here but has the services of Joao Moreira; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Japanese raider who posted career-best form when winning in UAE in February

runners.name: Harold Win
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season and has a tough draw today but scores good marks for consistency and has edged his way down to a good mark; usually races prominently; ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: Scores good marks for consistency and has edged down to a good mark

runners.name: Another World
runners.spotlight: Won three times over C&D last season and was also successful over 1m on dirt for new stable in December; needs to bounce back from last month's duff effort but current mark is workable.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from last month's duff effort but current mark is workable

runners.name: Daryl Flash
runners.spotlight: Smart when based in Australia but yet to be placed after five Hong Kong starts and might need further help from the local handicapper.
runners.comment: Smart when based in Australia but yet to make an impact in Hong Kong

runners.name: California Waves
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish third from stall 11 over C&D last month but his latest run was a shade disappointing and he's now 0-10 in Hong Kong; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Placed from wide stall over C&D last month but below that form since; now 0-10 in HK

runners.name: Helene Supafeeling
runners.spotlight: Scored over 6f here on Hong Kong debut in December; yet to make any progress from that performance but was a dual 1m winner when trained in Britain, so this move back up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: 6f winner on HK debut in December; today's move back up in trip ought to suit

runners.name: Chiu Chow Spirit
runners.spotlight: In the frame three times over C&D this season but looked out of sorts on last two appearances and has a wide draw today.
runners.comment: Looked out of sorts on last two outings and has a wide draw today
runners.owner: Charlie Lam Leung Seng & Michael Lam Yau Keng

runners.name: Positive Smile
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive C&D Class 4 races in January but has found things a bit tougher since upped to this grade and is drawn on the outside today.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in January but finding things a bit tougher since upped in grade

runners.name: The Red Hare
runners.spotlight: Placed on four of his five appearances since holding on for narrow C&D win in December, and benefits from Nichola Yuen's very useful 10lb today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Very consistent since narrow C&D win in December and good 10lb claimer booked
runners.owner: Lo Yu Sai, Jasmine Lo Siu Yu Et Al

runners.name: Aeroinvincible
runners.spotlight: Bang-in-form front-runner who has won his last two over C&D; had nothing to spare on latter occasion, though, and is 6lb higher in a higher-grade race today.
runners.comment: Won his last two over C&D but looks vulnerable off new mark in this higher grade

runners.name: Beauty Crescent
runners.spotlight: Reserve; began this season with two C&D wins but his form has slumped badly in recent months.
runners.comment: Began this season with two C&D wins but his form has slumped badly in recent months

runners.name: Fit For Beauty
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected three course wins (7f-1m) this season and was not discredited when second over C&D this month; ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Three wins this season (7f-1m); second over C&D this month; commands respect

runners.name: Complete Unknown
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ex-Australian 3yo who launched his Hong Kong with two encouraging runs, the latter a C&D fifth two weeks ago; needs to take a step forward today, though.
runners.comment: Launched his Hong Kong with two encouraging runs; needs a step forward today

runners.name: Mighty Commander
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner at Happy Valley this season; also suited by this C&D but was a disappointing favourite under Joao Moreira this month and remains on a fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner this season; also suited by 7f but others look better handicapped

runners.name: Legendary Years
runners.spotlight: Safely held on both starts for new stable this season (7f/1m) and has now been unplaced all five Hong Kong starts; probably needs a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five Hong Kong starts and needs a drop in grade

runners.name: Power Of Vitam
runners.spotlight: Left low-key Hong Kong debut behind when causing 46-1 surprise over C&D in February; not in same form here last month and not easy to predict today but remains of interest.
runners.comment: Surprise C&D winner on second of his three Hong Kong starts; enters calculations

runners.name: Class
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; a down-the-field outsider on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m).
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m)
runners.owner: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho

runners.name: Masquerade Ball
runners.spotlight: Gained his Group 1 breakthrough over today's trip at Tokyo in November and bettered that performance when very close second in Japan Cup over 1m4f there later that month; usually finishes well, and will be dangerous if he gets a good pace to aim at here.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over this trip in Japan in November; close second in 1m4f Japan Cup since

runners.name: Romantic Warrior
runners.spotlight: Outstanding 8yo who created history when winning a fourth consecutive Hong Kong Cup over C&D in December; has collected another two Group 1s since (1m/1m2f), taking tally at this level to 13; commands respect.
runners.comment: 13 Group 1 wins, including last four editions of Hong Kong Cup over C&D; commands respect

runners.name: Royal Champion
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing 8yo who was placed in last year's Irish Champion Stakes over today's trip and showed career-best form when emphatic Group 1 winner over slightly further in Saudi Arabia in February; ought to make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Late-maturing 8yo who was emphatic winner of Group 1 in Saudi Arabia in February
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Sosie
runners.spotlight: Longchamp regular who won two Group 1s there last spring (10.5f/1m4f, good to soft) and kept on for commendable third in Arc de Triomphe (1m4f, very soft) in October; proved he can cope with much faster ground when bagging 1m4f Hong Hong Vase here in December but would appeal more over that trip.
runners.comment: French raider; won 1m4f Hong Kong Vase in December but would appeal more over that trip

runners.name: Giovanni
runners.spotlight: Japanese raider; as good as ever when very close second in 1m2f Group 1 at Chukyo six weeks ago but probably needs to find hidden reserves here.
runners.comment: Very close second in Japanese Group 1 last month but probably needs to find extra here

runners.name: June Take
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 5yo who took Group 2 honours over 1m3f at Tokyo in February and was close fourth (just behind Giovanni) over this trip at that level last month; probably has a bit to find with the principals here, though.
runners.comment: Resurgent 5yo who took Group 2 honours at Tokyo in February; bit more needed here, though

runners.name: Rubylot
runners.spotlight: Smart on his day and versatile trip-wise but seems badly to have lost his way this season and has too much to prove; headgear switched.
runners.comment: Seems badly to have lost his way this season and has too much to prove
runners.owner: HK Football Club Horse Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Numbers
runners.spotlight: Smart when based in Australia and has 2-5 strike-rate in Hong Kong, having made all in 1m1f Group 3 handicap here in February; commendable second over C&D in last month's Hong Kong Derby but needs improvement today.
runners.comment: Commendable second over C&D in last month's Hong Kong Derby but needs improvement today
runners.owner: 23/24 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Family Jewel
runners.spotlight: Didn't fire over 1m2f last time but made the frame over C&D two starts ago and is now only 2lb higher than for pushed-out C&D win under Joao Moreira last April; could have a say.
runners.comment: Didn't fire over 1m2f last time but made the frame over C&D two runs ago; a possible
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor, Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Ghorgan
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed twice over 7f this season and his European form suggests this return to 1m will suit; however, he has questions to answer after two below-par efforts.
runners.comment: Placed twice over 7f this season but has questions to answer at the moment

runners.name: Master Trillion
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly when upped to this grade last month but held on to pip next-time-out winner The Golden Knight over C&D two starts ago (subsequently demoted to second) and has claims if judged on that performance.
runners.comment: Flopped last time but finished first over C&D two runs ago; claims if judged on that

runners.name: Natural Numbers
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong; placed at triple-digit odds over C&D in February but finished well down the field on last two outings; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Placed at triple-digit odds in February but well down the field on last two outings

runners.name: Windlord
runners.spotlight: Smart when trained in Britain but unplaced all five Hong Kong starts; only beaten about 2l when seventh over C&D this month but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Smart when trained in Britain but unplaced all five Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Dragon On Snow
runners.spotlight: Looked promising as a 2yo in Ireland but, following a long absence, launched Hong Kong career with two low-key 7f runs this year.
runners.comment: Launched Hong Kong career with two low-key 7f runs this year
runners.owner: Eddie Hon Hoi Kwong, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Rising Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner this season; also ran well over C&D on last two outings and is not yet fully exposed over this trip; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner this season; also ran well over C&D on last two outings
runners.owner: James Kwan Wai Ming & Leung Kam Chuen

runners.name: Trinity Treasure
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden on debut in Australia last year but struggled to get competitive when a big-priced outsider for last month's Hong Kong debut at Happy Valley (1m).
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive when a big-priced outsider on Hong Kong debut
runners.owner: Zhao Guoming, Xiao Bing, Annie An Ran & Tan Meng Sha

runners.name: Lovero
runners.spotlight: Placed twice since his C&D win in January and didn't enjoy the run of the race when only seventh here last time; Zac Purton keeps the ride; ought to make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Placed twice since his C&D win in January; ought to make presence felt again

runners.name: The Golden Knight
runners.spotlight: Just held on from Endued for C&D win in this grade last month and now bids to complete a hat-trick; 6lb rise asks quite a lot of him but he might still be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Just held on over C&D last month and now bids to complete a C&D hat-trick
runners.owner: Wilson Ngan Ping Shing, Katherine Ngan Mei Sze Et Al

runners.name: Shamus Storm
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to prevail over 1m1f here last month and is now 2-11; might be vulnerable after recent 6lb rise but the drop back in trip won't be a problem.
runners.comment: Just did enough to prevail over 1m1f here last month and is now 2-11
runners.owner: Lily Chan Li Lei & Marc Chan

runners.name: Endued
runners.spotlight: Just held on for C&D win last month and posted another career-best performance when very close second to The Golden Knight here three weeks later; had excuses when only ninth on latest outing (repeatedly short of room) and can be treated as though still in prime form.
runners.comment: Met traffic on latest outing; had previously been in career-best form; considered

runners.name: Blazing Wukong
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for clearcut C&D win in February but found things much tougher in this higher grade on last two appearances (1m/1m1f) and still needs to prove himself at this level.
runners.comment: Good C&D winner in February but has found things tough in this higher grade since

runners.name: The Boom Box
runners.spotlight: Reserve; scored at Happy Valley in early part of this season but recent efforts have been underwhelming; needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Won in early part of this season but recent efforts have been underwhelming

runners.name: Shanwah
runners.spotlight: Generally very disappointing during this first season in Hong Kong but took big step in right direction when close sixth over C&D this month and now has something to build upon; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Initially very disappointing Hong Kong but latest run was a big step forward

runners.name: Lucky Sam Gor
runners.spotlight: Began this year with two course wins (7f/1m) and kept on well for fourth off today's mark on latest outing last month (7f); could still have untapped potential over 1m; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Two wins this year; fourth over 7f here last time; still unexposed over 1m

races.race_name: FWD Insurance CMB Wing Lung Bank Handicap (Class 2) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Drombeg Banner
runners.spotlight: Gave pretty good account when fifth over C&D this month and recent 2lb drop is helpful but this season's record is rather mixed and he's drawn wide today; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ran well when fifth over C&D this month but this season's record is rather mixed
runners.owner: Clement Ma Kang Ching, Josiah Ma Pok Man & David Ma Yeuk Lai

runners.name: Mugen
runners.spotlight: Returned to form and caused 43-1 shock over C&D this month, leading close home to pip Winning Ovation; 6lb higher today but remains well handicapped on older form and Joao Moreira is booked.
runners.comment: Led close home for surprise C&D win this month; still well treated on older form

runners.name: Juneau Pride
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times behind Emblazon over C&D in January-March and not disgraced in Hong Kong Derby last time; probably won't be far away but others in this line-up may have more potential.
runners.comment: In good form over C&D this year but others in this field may have more potential

runners.name: Mighty Masts
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with emphatic C&D win this month; 9lb higher in a higher-grade race today but could still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Came good in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with emphatic C&D win this month
runners.owner: 24/25 Chris So Wai Yin Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Light Years Charm
runners.spotlight: Won five times over C&D in 2025 but has not been in quite the same form this year, albeit he kept on for a respectable fourth here last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won five times over C&D in 2025 but not quite in same form this year

runners.name: Kaholo Angel
runners.spotlight: Probably best known as a sprinter, although his latest win (in December) was gained over 1m; well below best since the turn of the year and far too inconsistent to rely upon nowadays.
runners.comment: Below best this year and far too inconsistent to rely upon nowadays
runners.owner: Ho Garrick Sai Man & Tam Wing Yee

runners.name: Beauty Bolt
runners.spotlight: Reserve; progressive over 1m this season and last month's defeat in Hong Kong Derby can probably be overlooked but this drop back in trip may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Progressive over 1m this season but this drop back in trip may not be ideal
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Infinite Resolve
runners.spotlight: Took form to new level with commendable second in Classic Mile here in February and wasn't beaten far in last month's Classic Cup (1m1f) but managed only sixth over C&D when back in a handicap three weeks later and needs to kick on again to overcome current mark.
runners.comment: Second in Classic Mile in February but only sixth over C&D when back in a handicap

runners.name: Emblazon
runners.spotlight: Ran as though something was amiss when upped to 1m2f for last month's Hong Kong Derby but won four consecutive handicaps (three over C&D) beforehand; can feature if back on song, despite his wide draw.
runners.comment: Tailed off in last month's HK Derby but won four consecutive handicaps beforehand

runners.name: Six Pack
runners.spotlight: In the frame four consecutive times since narrow C&D win in January, most recently when very close third behind Mugen and Winning Ovation over C&D this month; nudged up 2lb to another career-high mark today but has the inside stall again; respected.
runners.comment: In the frame four consecutive times since narrow C&D win in January; respected

runners.name: Winning Ovation
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who won three in a row over C&D before his short-head second to Mugen this month; 4lb better off with him today and has a better draw; likely contender.
runners.comment: Won three in a row over C&D before this month's close second; likely contender again

runners.name: Akashvani
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won from the front over C&D in January and is not yet fully exposed over this trip but managed only sixth off his new mark in recent C&D event won by Mugen.
runners.comment: Won from the front over C&D in January but held off new mark since

runners.name: Salon S
runners.spotlight: Exciting front-runner who is unbeaten after four starts, the last three over C&D; moves up in grade today but it remains very hard to gauge the limit of his ability; must be considered.
runners.comment: Exciting front-runner who is unbeaten after four starts; must be considered
runners.owner: 24/25 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Packing Hermod
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who made the frame in a C&D Group 3 handicap in the early part of this season; on a workable mark and can easily be excused his Group 1 defeat in February but stall 13 makes things awkward.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins; back on a workable mark but stall 1 makes things awkward

runners.name: We Are Hero
runners.spotlight: Gained all six Hong Kong wins over 5f here, the latest a dead-heat last month; unraced beyond 6f since his debut in Australia in 2021, and looks opposable over this trip.
runners.comment: Dead-heated over 5f here last month but looks opposable over this trip

runners.name: Storm Rider
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season, the most notable a last-gasp success in C&D Group 3 handicap in January; this month's 1m Group 2 defeat is not viewed harshly but he might be a shade too high in the weights for this handicap return.
runners.comment: Won C&D Group 3 handicap in January but may be too high in the weights now

runners.name: Double Major
runners.spotlight: Two-time Group 1 winner, both the same race but at different venues, one of them here over 110yds further; retains his ability but has found Asmarani too good both times this season, although this time he gives away a lot less weight; should be involved in the finish if at his best and Stephane Pasquier 1-1 on him.
runners.comment: Dual Group 1 winner; beaten twice by Asmarani this year but weighted to beat him this time

runners.name: Presage Nocturne
runners.spotlight: Won this last year and went on to run well in plenty of good races after, notably the Caulfield Cup in October, which led him to go off market leader for the Melbourne Cup after that; wasn't beaten far in Saudi Arabia in February and no doubt this race has been a target; Christophe Soumillon on for first time in over a year.
runners.comment: Won this race last year, so this is probably his early-season target
runners.owner: P Zambelli, Ms R Khaddam Et Al

runners.name: Espoir Avenir
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout a lengthy 3yo campaign, winning twice, once over this C&D when landing the Group 3 Prix Gerald de Geoffre on very soft; finished a place ahead of Presage Nocturne in Saudi Arabia in February, albeit getting 6lb, but was comfortably held last month in a 1m4f Listed contest; needs step back up in trip and hood to see him come back to form.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has not been at his best recently; hood goes on so may do better

runners.name: Asmarani
runners.spotlight: Very useful sort at up to 1m7f in 2025 but has taken his form to the next level this campaign having been gelded, beating Double Major the last two times, most recently over the same distance at Saint-Cloud on soft; shouldn't beat that rival on these terms but does have more scope for improvement.
runners.comment: Much improved since gelded; needs more to beat Double Major again but has scope

runners.name: Daryz
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his first run on fast ground in the International at York last August and short-headed in the Prix D'Orange but won all his other starts and posted a clear pb upped to 1m4f on very soft ground on his final start when beating multiple Group 1 winner Minnie Hauk (pair clear) in the Arc; off since but has reportedly thrived physically; a muddling gallop over this trip on less-testing ground isn't certain to suit but he's capable of better this year and is a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Clear pb when winning the Arc; can do better but muddling gallop over this trip a worry

runners.name: First Look
runners.spotlight: Has won four of his 14 starts including Group 2/3 wins in second half of last year; has twice finished behind Bright Picture this spring but it should be close between the pair now they're meeting at level weights; can make the running.
runners.comment: Dual Group winner in 2025 and closely matched with Bright Picture on this year's form

runners.name: Bright Picture
runners.spotlight: Improved performer this year and followed his Group 3 Exbury win with a narrow success over Leffard in the Group 2 Harcourt; closely matched at the weights with stable companion First Look (fourth) on that form but he's capable of better, he won't mind further rain and he's well worth his place in this company.
runners.comment: Improved this year with wins in the Exbury and Harcourt; well worth his place in a Group 1

runners.name: Aventure
runners.spotlight: High-class mare who won three of her five starts last year, including a first Group 1 in the Vermeille; was a dual winner around this trip last season and, although she disappointed when last seen in the Arc, she goes well fresh and should make a bold bid to become the first filly/mare to win this since Aquarelliste in 2002.
runners.comment: Bagged a first Group 1 in last year's Vermeille and effective at 1m2f; likely to run well

runners.name: Arrow Eagle
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year, respectable sixth in the Arc but bagged a first Group 1 success when winning the Royal-Oak on very soft ground at Saint-Cloud in autumn; off since but hard to think a potentially messy race over this trip on less-testing ground will play to his strengths, even if he sets out to make it a stamina test.
runners.comment: Bagged first Group 1 when taking the Royal-Oak (over just short of 2m); this trip a worry

races.race_name: Sky Bet Proud To Support Young Lives Vs Cancer Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Dwindling Funds
runners.spotlight: Six wins last July-October (various ground) and campaign concluded with a near-miss over C&D; continued the good work when runner-up in a recent 0-95 here (1m1f, good) and he has leading claims back down in grade.
runners.comment: Six wins last July-October and reappeared with fine second here; leading claims
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: In good form on the Newcastle AW this year but not at the same level back on turf here (1m1f, good) a fortnight ago and now 0-11 on grass.
runners.comment: In good form on Newcastle AW this year but not at same level back on turf here recently

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Runner-up five times last May-August and he's on an attractive mark on that form; he may have needed last week's reappearance run at Ripon but was well beaten, which was also the case on his final few outings last season.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark but went off the boil at end of last season, and well beaten on return

runners.name: Eazy On The Eye
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on turf and hanging right on AW has been costly on her last two starts, being demoted from first at Wolverhampton (8.6f) then finishing third at Newcastle (1m; raced mostly alone); however, this front-runner arrives in good heart and has shown promise on grass; could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: 0-7 on turf but this front-runner has been in good form on AW & has shown promise on grass

runners.name: Madame De Sevigne
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but she returned to form with a close third at Lingfield (7f, AW) this month on her third stable start; she's fine at 1m and on turf and can be in the mix if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Back to form with close third at Lingfield and in the mix if backing up that performance

runners.name: Harry The Rogue
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from his 33 starts (both over 1m last spring on good/yielding) but he was highly consistent last year and ran another big race when going close on last month's stable debut at Newcastle (1m, AW); every chance he'll be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Just 2-33 but admirably consistent and went close on last month's stable debut

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Below par last October on last two runs for Dermot Weld and failed to threaten on recent stable/seasonal debut at Wolverhampton, but that run gives him something to build on and he's racing off his last winning mark today.
runners.comment: Reappearance run gives him something to build on and he's racing off his last winning mark

runners.name: Ashen
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and has come seventh on his three starts for this yard (7f/6f); however, he hasn't been running badly, with his latest effort coming in a big field at Thirsk, and there have been hints that his return to 1m may suit.
runners.comment: 0-10 but not running badly for this yard and it's possible the return to 1m will suit

runners.name: Swift Salian
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row last May/June (8.3f/1m1f, good/soft) and beaten just a head on this month's reappearance at Pontefract (1m, good to soft); this front-runner has a progressive profile and is one to consider today.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has a progressive profile and went close at Pontefract on reappearance

runners.name: Ayr Poet
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was as good as ever last season, winning three times, including at Ayr in September when last season; this two-time course winner is not ruled out on his return but there's a suspicion he'll improve for the run.
runners.comment: 11yo who won three times last season but there's a suspicion he'll improve for this run
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Two B Tanned
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won five times last April-July (good/good to firm), including over C&D; down the field at Nottingham last October when last seen but this mark isn't necessarily beyond her in view of her good third at Haydock (1m, good) in September; could make a bold bid if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Five wins last April-July and current mark not necessarily beyond her; reappearance

runners.name: Detective
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has an up-and-down profile last year and this is his first run since September; however, he was a good third over C&D (good) on his reappearance last April and won here (1m1f, good) in July; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: 10yo who returns from some time off but was third over C&D on his reappearance last April

runners.name: Native Honey
runners.spotlight: Last season's high points were his second at Doncaster (1m, good) in May and a win at Ayr (7f, good) in August; however, he's inconsistent and has to be at the very top of his game to win this on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Won last August but has to be at the very top of his game to win this on his reappearance

runners.name: Telling Time
runners.spotlight: Best form when placed twice on Wolverhampton AW; only midfield back on turf (good to soft) last time but may do better back on faster ground.
runners.comment: May do better back on faster ground

runners.name: Elashgar
runners.spotlight: Successful over 6f and keeping-on second over 5f at Wolverhampton kept to AW since handicapping; gelded since; remains open to further progress back up in distance; respected, assuming he's as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Remains open to further progress back up in distance on turf debut

runners.name: Faithful Dream
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but has several pieces of encouraging form, most recently third of 17 in cheekpieces at Nottingham (5f) last weekend; effective over 6f and has possibilities if taking well to visor.
runners.comment: Respectable third of 17 at Nottingham last weekend; possibilities

runners.name: Petes Diamond
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who is far from solid on form; achieved little on turf debut (7f) last time.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but is in form, fifth of 17 at Leicester (7f) returned to turf last time; could go well off the same mark.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but is in form, fifth of 17 at Leicester last time

runners.name: Noble Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on his AW maiden form but may do better now handicapping off a modest mark on turf.
runners.comment: May do better now handicapping and switched to turf

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on account-opening Chelmsford win kept to AW since and chance depends on the effects of this return to turf.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of this return to turf

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-18 and she looks fairly exposed, at least on AW; placed in two of her three turf runs (maiden/novice company).
runners.comment: Looks fairly exposed, at least on AW; placed twice on turf

runners.name: In Denial
runners.spotlight: Good fourth at Wolverhampton and hindered by wide trip at Chelmsford kept to AW since handicapping; may do better still at this sort of level; interesting, assuming she's as effective on turf.
runners.comment: May do better still at this sort of level; turf debut

runners.name: Ganthorpe
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has been campaigned mostly on AW, running well on penultimate start; siblings include a dual turf winner for his connections; interesting back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests he's interesting back on turf

runners.name: Bold Return
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has made the frame kept to 5f in her two handicap attempts, latest at Southwell (AW) on seasonal debut; in the mix, assuming she proves as effective at this new trip.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in her two handicap attempts; steps up to 6f

runners.name: Rupert Ritzik
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has achieved little; first-time headgear needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: First-time headgear needs to make a big difference
runners.owner: A Dearing, D Jenkins, T Bradshaw N Watts

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: Maiden who showed modest form for Aidan O'Brien and has even weaker claims on his AW efforts for new yard.
runners.comment: Maiden who needs to raise his game back on turf

runners.name: Sea Her Excel
runners.spotlight: Not totally solid on 2yo form but had wind surgery since and now goes in hood; those factors may prompt progress on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery since last run and now goes in hood; may progress

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Regressive RPRs in Southwell AW novice events; chance depends on how well she responds to new scenario and first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Regressive RPRs in Southwell AW novice events

races.race_name: Enter ITV 7 For Free EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Seed Ya Later
runners.spotlight: Her dam is from a talented family and she recovered from a slow start to finish a clear second of 13 at 10-1 on last Friday's debut at Bath (5f, good); could be sharper for that run and she's a leading candidate.
runners.comment: Clear second on debut at Bath and could be sharper for that run; leading candidate

runners.name: Donna Rumma
runners.spotlight: Beaten 4l by the 11-8 winner on recent debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) but was sent off just 7-2 herself and would have finished closer with a clear run; the third has finished second since; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: 4l second on recent debut at Beverley and she'd have been closer with a clear run

runners.name: Pocket Change
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; fourth foal; Far Above half-sister to 2m1f/2m2f hurdle winner Lynches Knock; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 63), half-sister to 6f-1m3f winner The Blue Banana; a watching brief may prove best on debut.
runners.comment: 3,000euros yearling; half-sister to a hurdle winner; a watching brief may prove best
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 13 & P J Makin

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: 7,000gns yearling, resold £15,000 yearling; third foal; Caturra half-brother to 6f AW winner Fair Y Isle (RPR 71); dam multiple 5f winner (87), from useful family; it's likely he'll improve for this debut outing.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f AW winner Fair Y Isle (RPR 71); likely he'll improve for this debut

runners.name: Caturra Lights
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal, 42,000gns yearling; fourth foal; Caturra half-brother to 6f winner Brigid's Cloak (RPR 89); dam 1m/1m2f winner (78), half-sister to numerous winners; makes debut against rivals who have already shown considerable promise and he'll need to be useful.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f winner Brigid's Cloak (RPR 89); makes debut in warm-looking maiden

runners.name: Tamam Star
runners.spotlight: 27,000euros yearling; ninth foal; Kodi Bear brother to 6f/7f AW winner Night Narcissus (RPR 94), half-brother to four winners including Ascending (1m-2m4f; 107), Midnight Malibu (5f including 2yo; 99) and Mr Pocket (5f/6f including 2yo; 84); dam Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner (97); trainer has an excellent strike-rate here with 2yos and he's respected on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer has an excellent strike-rate here with 2yos and he's respected on debut

runners.name: Super Alpha
runners.spotlight: 8-1, no match for the easy winner when third over C&D (soft) three weeks ago on debut; however, he shaped as though he would improve for that experience and he has a classy pedigree; not ruled out.
runners.comment: No match for easy winner when third over C&D on debut, but likely to improve

runners.name: Stripes Of Glory
runners.spotlight: 10-1 when sixth of 11, beaten just over 4l, on recent debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft); her dam was a Listed-placed 2yo winner and that initial effort gives her something to build on; not discounted.
runners.comment: Her dam was a useful 2yo and her recent debut at Beverley gives her something to build on

runners.name: Layla Liz
runners.spotlight: Successful at Leicester on 2yo debut and solid record (2121) on Wolverhampton AW since, making all to beat Nad Alshiba Green on reappearance (5f) most recently; progressive filly who warrants respect.
runners.comment: Made all to beat Nad Alshiba Green at Wolverhampton on reappearance; progressive

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Green
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, including over C&D, for previous yard; close second to Layla Liz when favourite at Wolverhampton (5f) on AW/stable/seasonal debut, doing well having come from a difficult position; could well reverse the placings and go one better.
runners.comment: Close second to Layla Liz at Wolverhampton on stable/seasonal debut; has won over C&D

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Got up on the line at Southwell (5f) last time, taking AW record to 6-20; faces a slightly tougher assignment off revised mark back up in grade and back on turf; 2-10 in this sphere.
runners.comment: Southwell win last time took AW record to 6-20; he's 2-10 on turf
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Has returned to AW for two starts this year, better effort when runner-up on reappearance; has gained both turf wins at Windsor but is attractively treated off current mark; could go well.
runners.comment: Has gained both turf wins at Windsor but is attractively treated off current mark

runners.name: Smart Vision
runners.spotlight: Best to forgive reappearance effort (badly hampered); registered three wins apiece in 2024 and 2025 but needs to prove he can defy current mark.
runners.comment: Best to forgive reappearance effort but current mark looks difficult
runners.owner: J E Abbey And New Vision Bloodstock

runners.name: Michaela's Boy
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and it's four years since his sole success in this sphere; not the percentage call in this first grass attempt since last August.
runners.comment: Four years since sole success on turf; others preferred

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and has done all his winning over 6f/7f; inconsistent since last success; 0-12 over 5f and doesn't look the answer.
runners.comment: All wins at 6f/7f, mostly on AW; 0-12 over 5f

runners.name: Grandlad
runners.spotlight: Had an inconsistent spell on AW towards the end of 2025; has a better strike-rate on turf and recorded last year's August double off slightly higher marks; possibilities returned to this sphere on reappearance.
runners.comment: Recorded last year's August double off slightly higher marks; possibilities

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly in Bahrain since early 2025; far from solid on his domestic form since winning at Newmarket two years ago; no certainty to defy top weight.
runners.comment: Bahrain regular; far from solid on his domestic form since a win two years ago

runners.name: Muker
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner but reappearance record suggests he's probably best watched on this seasonal debut, unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Reappearance record suggests he's probably best watched

runners.name: Epidavros
runners.spotlight: 0-12 last year but often ran well, including over C&D, and she reappeared with a solid fourth here (1m, good) a fortnight ago; has a chance.
runners.comment: Often ran well last season, including over C&D; solid fourth here on reappearance; chance

runners.name: Sovereign Bright
runners.spotlight: Convincingly beaten when third on both outings this year (1m/1m2f, AW) and she's 0-8; however, she's run just twice on turf and the first occasion produced her career-best RPR, when going close at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last June; the other run on grass was on heavy going last July; the return to turf could be a major positive for this 3yo.
runners.comment: 0-8 and needs to raise her game but the return to turf could be a major positive

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Continues to drop down the weights and ran okay when third in January last time; another 3lb lower today but she's been beaten on her 15 starts since winning in December 2024 on her second run; hard to be overly confident.
runners.comment: Continues to drop down weights but losing sequence up to 15; hard to be overly confident

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Two wins on AW towards end of last year (1m2f/1m4f, AW) and she's had a break since a lesser run in January; drops back in trip and needs to prove she's effective on turf, but she hasn't had much racing and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Improved form on AW late last year; not ruled out back on turf and back down in trip

runners.name: Salaria
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-7 since joining Charlie Clover when winning at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month; has also won over 1m2f on turf; disappointing favourite at Pontefract since but was restless in the stalls; may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Prolific since joining this yard; disappointing last time but was restless in the stalls

runners.name: Glorious Kitty
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but unexposed at this sort of trip; twice finished runner-up last October (1m/8.3f, AW/good) and reappeared with close second at Beverley (8.4f, good to soft) recently; she can be thereabouts once more.
runners.comment: 0-13 but unexposed at this sort of trip and went close at Beverley on reappearance

runners.name: Lady Dora Mae
runners.spotlight: Won Southwell novice (1m, AW) in January and followed up on handicap debut at Kempton (7f, AW); eighth of 12 at Lingfield (7f, AW) since but beaten only about 2l and this lightly raced 3yo may still have more to offer, with her pedigree hinting that this step up in trip may well be a plus.
runners.comment: Unplaced at Lingfield in hat-trick bid but the step up in trip may well be a plus

runners.name: Fille Imbassee
runners.spotlight: Won on nursery debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in January on final start for Marco Botti; however, she needs to produce more than she's managed this spring on her first two outings for this yard; turf debut.
runners.comment: Won AW nursery in January but hasn't kicked on for this yard subsequently

runners.name: Samra Star
runners.spotlight: Well treated on this year's AW form; however, she has an up-and-down profile and has a lower turf mark for good reason on previous evidence.
runners.comment: Well treated on this year's AW form but has a lower turf mark for good reason

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in nurseries last autumn and only seventh of 12 on this month's reappearance at Nottingham; however, she won a nursery at Ripon (1m, good) last September and could improve for her recent run; not written off.
runners.comment: Only 7th of 12 on reappearance but could improve for it; won Ripon nursery last September

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-9 when 6l seventh of ten on stable debut here (7f, good) a fortnight ago; however, she may have needed that run and she ran well at up to 1m2f when with Harry Charlton; interesting now back up in trip.
runners.comment: She may have needed her recent stable debut; interesting now back up in trip

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: Won at Ayr (1m5f, good) last July and this month's reappearance second at Catterick was over 1m4f; drops back to 1m1f here but she's shown versatility in terms of trip in the past and could again be involved at the finish.
runners.comment: 1m4f runner-up on reappearance; tackles 1m1f here but can be versatile in terms of trip

runners.name: Storm On Jura
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has raced only five times but she was well beaten last autumn in her first two handicaps; a transformation is needed on her return.
runners.comment: 4yo who has raced only five times but well beaten last autumn in her first two handicaps

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Nottinghamshire Oaks Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: See The Fire
runners.spotlight: Smart filly who is a Group 3 and Group 2 winner at York (1m1f/1m2f); has plenty of Group 1 form, including placed efforts at Royal Ascot (against males) and Longchamp (close second) last season; takes on some unexposed and interesting rivals but sets a high standard for them to aim at; respected back down sharply in class.
runners.comment: Smart filly; Group 3 and Group 2 winner; has plenty of Group 1 form; sets a high standard

runners.name: Love Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo who has been kept to autumn races on soft/heavy ground; something to prove in this new scenario but otherwise an interesting type, having gained her 3yo success at Nottingham (1m) and being still open to further improvement; ran well in Listed contests the last twice and gives the impression she's ready for a crack at this new trip.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced; gained her 3yo win at Nottingham; open to further improvement

runners.name: Lava Stream
runners.spotlight: Absent since a 2024 campaign that ended with her coming up short in the Irish Oaks and Yorkshire Oaks; progressive earlier and went close in the Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot (1m4f) when bidding for a four-timer; could go well, provided she returns in good order.
runners.comment: Absent since a 2024 campaign that featured a close second in the Ribblesdale

runners.name: It's A Heartbeat
runners.spotlight: Irish filly who has very low mileage; completed a Curragh double (maiden/handicap) last summer; reappearance effort in Cork Listed event (kept to 1m4f) suggests this drop back in trip is worth exploring; may rate higher still; interesting.
runners.comment: Irish filly who has very low mileage; may rate higher still; interesting

runners.name: That's Amore
runners.spotlight: Similar type to Love Dynasty, being very lightly raced and one who may still be capable of bigger figures; fourth in the 2025 Cheshire Oaks is flanked by novice wins, latest at Southwell (1m3f, AW) on seasonal debut; likely has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Record is 2-3; improvement still possible; has a better chance than ratings suggest

runners.name: Alla Stella
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form when making the frame in Sandown Listed event and Curragh Group 3; one of two contestants who have been absent since 2024; needs improvement on ratings; market should offer further guidance.
runners.comment: One of two contestants who have been absent since 2024; check the betting

races.race_name: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Close third at Beverley (8.5f) 11 days ago and she's won twice there at 1m2f, but her record at 1m3f-1m4f (six races) is less convincing.
runners.comment: Close third (8.5f) 11 days ago; two 1m2f wins but record at 1m3f-1m4f is less convincing

runners.name: Furhaan
runners.spotlight: Won handicap debut at Windsor (11.5f, heavy) in September and creditable fifth at Yarmouth (11.5f, good) the following month; left Ed Walker for 32,000gns in October and last week's reappearance (25-1) did not suggest an imminent return to winning form.
runners.comment: Last week's seasonal/yard debut (25-1) did not suggest an imminent return to winning form

runners.name: Night Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won off just 1lb lower at Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) last August, his second win there last year; 0-12 on AW but close third on latest start (first with tongue tied) was up with his best form; should go well.
runners.comment: Two 1m4f wins at Ascot (good to firm) last year; latest AW run was up with his best form

runners.name: Fast Fred
runners.spotlight: Second at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) on reappearance and better than ever when taking a 14-runner handicap over C&D (good) two weeks ago; 4lb higher this time but firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Better than ever when taking a 14-runner handicap over C&D (good) two weeks ago; up 4lb

runners.name: Hamlet's Night
runners.spotlight: Won on Kempton AW (1m3f; not proven over this far) in February and latest start was up with his best hurdles form; 0-13 on Flat turf but that includes five seconds.
runners.comment: AW win in February; good hurdles run latest; 0-13 on Flat turf but that includes five 2nds

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Made her 15th start, with tongue-tie and blinkers replacing cheekpieces, when getting off the mark in a nine-runner handicap at Dundalk (10.5f, AW) one month ago, doing it in clearcut style under 7lb claimer; has comparable form on good ground but not proven over this far (tried it on penultimate start) and she's 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 15th go when off the mark latest (10.5f, AW); 4lb out of the handicap and stamina unproven

runners.name: Motawaared
runners.spotlight: Down the field for George Boughey last May/June in his first two handicaps (1m4f/1m2f) and sold for 12,000gns in July; beaten further still at Ayr (1m) in September on his only start for new yard; moves back up in trip and needs to tap back into his initial promise.
runners.comment: Down the field in 3 handicaps, one for this yard; needs to tap back into initial promise

runners.name: Letsbefrank
runners.spotlight: Three wins (1m3f, 1m6f and 2m1f) in summer 2024 and he's 7lb below his last winning mark; struggled to get competitive last year until his running-on fifth under Lauren Young in a blanket finish on final start (1m4f, soft); reappearance record isn't great and today's test may be a bit too sharp for him.
runners.comment: Reappearance record isn't great and today's test may be a bit too sharp for him

runners.name: Eroico
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Irish maidens, taking minor honours on last three outings (1m2f/1m4f on softer than good); has since changed hands and left Noel Meade; improvement is probably needed on this British, seasonal and handicap debut.
runners.comment: 0-5 in Irish maidens, taking minor honours on last three outings (1m2f/1m4f); left N Meade

runners.name: Rock N Roll Pinkie
runners.spotlight: 3-10 at Nottingham, including the first of last year's three wins; effective at 1m6f/2m and ran well on recent return, so shortlisted.
runners.comment: 3-10 at Nottingham; effective at 1m6f/2m and ran well on recent return

runners.name: Macari
runners.spotlight: An excellent Flat campaign last year yielded three turf wins under Rose Dawes from May to October and he's been in AW/hurdles action this winter; absent 11 weeks but has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Excellent Flat campaign last year; in AW/hurdles action this winter; absent 11 weeks
runners.owner: R Vaughan, R Bisiker, M Jones And S West

runners.name: Just An Hour
